For on-policy scenarios, if you prefer not to explicitly construct ⟦INLMATH511MATHEND⟧, directly using ⟦INLMATH512MATHEND⟧ is simpler and less error-prone.

  2. If you want KL to be part of the shaped reward — so it flows through return / advantage construction together with the task reward — choose KL as reward shaping, using ⟦INLMATH513MATHEND⟧.

Based on the above conclusions about “value unbiasedness vs. gradient correctness” and “differences between Loss and Reward implementations”, we now proceed to the quick reference guide and common pitfalls that can be directly applied to code.

9. Practical Guide and Common Pitfalls

9.1 Quick Reference for the Three Estimator Definitions

⟦DISPMATH70MATHEND⟧

9.2 Value Estimation Properties

Estimator Unbiased for reverse KL ⟦INLMATH514MATHEND⟧ value? Variance
⟦INLMATH515MATHEND⟧ ✓ High (can be negative)
⟦INLMATH516MATHEND⟧ ✗ (often small near KL=0, not exact) Low
⟦INLMATH517MATHEND⟧ ✓ Low

9.3 Common Pitfalls

  1. Using ⟦INLMATH518MATHEND⟧ directly as a loss term (on-policy): Gradient expectation is zero, completely ineffective.
  2. Using ⟦INLMATH519MATHEND⟧ as a loss term to optimize reverse KL (on-policy): Its gradient corresponds to forward KL ⟦INLMATH520MATHEND⟧, i.e. the wrong direction.
  3. Using ⟦INLMATH521MATHEND⟧ (importance weight not detached) off-policy: Gradient corresponds to a local second-order surrogate, not reverse KL.
  4. Using ⟦INLMATH522MATHEND⟧ inside reward shaping: Although it is value-unbiased, it induces a biased policy-gradient update and may lead to training collapse.
  5. Simply setting ⟦INLMATH523MATHEND⟧ to constant 1 in on-policy: Must explicitly construct ⟦INLMATH524MATHEND⟧ (or equivalently ⟦INLMATH525MATHEND⟧), otherwise the score-function gradient path is lost, causing ⟦INLMATH526MATHEND⟧ and ⟦INLMATH527MATHEND⟧ to degenerate to naive forms and fail.
  6. Confusing “value unbiasedness” with “gradient correctness”: ⟦INLMATH528MATHEND⟧ is value-unbiased for reverse KL, but when used in reward shaping, the induced policy gradient is biased; both dimensions matter.

10. Summary

If you only remember four lines, make them these:

  1. Value unbiasedness does not imply gradient correctness. Choosing a KL estimator means checking not only how well it estimates KL values, but also what objective its gradient is actually optimizing.
  2. If KL is a differentiable loss term: in the naive on-policy implementation, ⟦INLMATH529MATHEND⟧ is the simplest correct choice; if you explicitly construct ⟦INLMATH530MATHEND⟧, or if you are off-policy, prefer ⟦INLMATH531MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH532MATHEND⟧.
  3. If KL is used as stop-gradient reward shaping: in the policy-gradient term analyzed here, only ⟦INLMATH533MATHEND⟧ stays aligned with reverse-KL regularization.
  4. A low-variance KL loss and ⟦INLMATH534MATHEND⟧ in reward shaping can be sample-wise equivalent on the KL term, but the two algorithms still have different semantics. In the former, KL is an explicit regularizer; in the latter, KL flows through advantage, baselines, and credit assignment.

11. References

  1. Dibya Ghosh. “KL Divergence for Machine Learning”. https://dibyaghosh.com/blog/probability/kldivergence
  2. John Schulman. “Approximating KL Divergence”. https://joschu.net/blog/kl-approx.html
  3. Verl Documentation. “Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)”. https://verl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/algo/ppo.html
  4. 初七123334. “RLHF/RLVR 训练中的 KL 近似方法浅析（k1 / k2 / k3）”. https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/1966872846212010437
  5. Kezhao Liu, Jason Klein Liu, Mingtao Chen, Yiming Liu. “Rethinking KL Regularization in RLHF: From Value Estimation to Gradient Optimization”. arXiv:2510.01555. https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.01555
  6. Yifan Zhang, Yiping Ji, Gavin Brown, et al. “On the Design of KL-Regularized Policy Gradient Algorithms for LLM Reasoning”. arXiv:2505.17508. https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.17508
  7. Vedant Shah, Johan Obando-Ceron, Vineet Jain, Brian Bartoldson, Bhavya Kailkhura, Sarthak Mittal, Glen Berseth, Pablo Samuel Castro. “A Comedy of Estimators: On KL Regularization in RL Training of LLMs”. arXiv:2512.21852. https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.21852
@misc{WangZhang2025KLEstimators,
  author       = {Wang, Xihuai and Zhang, Shao},
  title        = {Choosing KL Estimators in RL: From Value Unbiasedness to Gradient Correctness},
  year         = {2025},
  month        = dec,
  day          = {01},
  url          = {https://xihuai18.github.io/reinforcement-learning/2025/12/01/kl-estimators-en.html}
}
entry.summary: In RL, KL estimators should not be judged only by how accurately they estimate KL values, but also by what objective their gradients actually optimize. This post compares k1, k2, k3 in on-policy and off-policy settings, and turns the result into a practical selection guide.

entry.title: RL 中的 KL 估计器选型：从数值无偏到梯度正确

link.title: RL 中的 KL 估计器选型：从数值无偏到梯度正确

entry.content:
在强化学习中，KL 估计器不能只看数值估得准不准，还要看它在 loss 或 reward 写法下究竟优化了谁。本文比较三种经典估计器 ⟦INLMATH71MATHEND⟧ 在 on-policy 和 off-policy 场景下的性质差异，并说明当 KL 作为可微损失项或 detached 的奖励塑形项时，选型结论会怎样变化。

1. 引言：KL 散度（Kullback-Leibler 散度）在强化学习中的角色

这篇文章只想回答一个实现层面的问题：同样都写着”加 KL 惩罚”，为什么换一个估计器、换一个采样分布，或只是多加一个 .detach()，优化目标就可能已经变了？在策略优化算法（如 PPO、GRPO）或对齐训练框架（如 RLHF、RLAIF）中，KL 惩罚项看似只是个稳定训练的正则项；但一旦落到代码层面，实现方式就分出好几个维度：估计器的选择（⟦INLMATH72MATHEND⟧、⟦INLMATH73MATHEND⟧、⟦INLMATH74MATHEND⟧）、采样分布的选择（on-policy 或 off-policy），以及 KL 项的用法（作为损失项直接反传，还是作为奖励塑形项）。

要把这个问题讲清楚，得分开两件事：一是”估计某个 KL 的数值”，二是”反向传播时实际在优化哪个目标”。很多实现恰恰就是在这两件事上把概念混在了一起。

1.1 正向 KL 与反向 KL 的区别

设 ⟦INLMATH75MATHEND⟧ 为当前 actor 策略，⟦INLMATH76MATHEND⟧ 为参考策略，两种方向的 KL 散度分别为：

反向 KL（Reverse KL）：

⟦DISPMATH1MATHEND⟧

正向 KL（Forward KL）：

⟦DISPMATH2MATHEND⟧

直观解释：

  • 反向 KL 是 mode-seeking 的：优化后的策略会集中到参考分布的高概率区域，多样性可能降低。
  • 正向 KL 是 mass-covering 的：策略会尽量铺满参考分布的整个支撑集。

RLHF 的主流实现里更常用 反向 KL，因为通常只希望 actor 策略不要过度偏离参考策略，而不是要求它完全覆盖参考分布的所有模式。

1.2 三个会改变结论的选择

实现 KL 惩罚时的分歧可以归为三个选择：样本从谁来、约束哪个方向的 KL、KL 项是直接反传还是只做 reward 的系数。任意切换其中一个，推荐的估计器就可能变。

  1. 采样来源：样本来自当前策略 ⟦INLMATH77MATHEND⟧（on-policy），还是行为策略 ⟦INLMATH78MATHEND⟧（off-policy）？
  2. 估计目标：要估的是反向 KL ⟦INLMATH79MATHEND⟧，还是正向 KL ⟦INLMATH80MATHEND⟧？
  3. 应用方式：KL 项作为损失的一部分参与反向传播，还是作为奖励塑形项（加 stop-gradient）？

本文范围：主要讨论 token/sample 级 KL 项及其在 policy-gradient 主项中的行为。Critic、GAE、baseline 归一化，以及一般多步 MDP 的严格 off-policy 修正，只在相关处简要说明，不做系统展开。

与主要讨论 KL 数值近似的经典笔记不同，本文更关心近期 LLM-RL 文献反复提到的那个问题：同一个估计器一旦从 reward 系数改成可微损失项，梯度到底还在不在优化你以为的目标。

先看结论（只针对本文讨论的 token/sample 级 KL 项）

  • 若目标是反向 KL，且把 KL 写成可微损失项：on-policy 的朴素写法直接用 ⟦INLMATH81MATHEND⟧ 最省心；若显式构造 ⟦INLMATH82MATHEND⟧，则推荐 ⟦INLMATH83MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH84MATHEND⟧。
  • 若把 KL 写成 stop-grad reward shaping：就策略梯度主项而言，只有 ⟦INLMATH85MATHEND⟧ 保持与反向 KL 正则一致的无偏梯度。
  • 其余常见配置的问题通常不是”数值偏差稍大”，而是梯度目标已经变了。

2. 选型速查表（可跳读）

下面三张表是全文最核心的操作结论。先给出一页符号预备，读者可以据此在对应场景里直接选出推荐写法，再按需回到后面相应章节看推导。

符号速览（完整定义见下一章）：

  • ⟦INLMATH86MATHEND⟧：当前策略；⟦INLMATH87MATHEND⟧：参考策略；⟦INLMATH88MATHEND⟧：采样策略（on-policy 时 ⟦INLMATH89MATHEND⟧）。
  • ⟦INLMATH90MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH91MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH92MATHEND⟧。
  • ⟦INLMATH93MATHEND⟧：统一重要性权重；⟦INLMATH94MATHEND⟧ 表示 stop-gradient。

2.1 On-policy 下把反向 KL 写成损失项

损失项写法 优点 问题 推荐
⟦INLMATH95MATHEND⟧ — 梯度期望为零，完全无效，不能用于优化 ✗✗
⟦INLMATH96MATHEND⟧ 梯度正确（反向 KL），低方差，实现最简单 数值有偏（小 KL 邻域通常偏差较小） ✓✓
⟦INLMATH97MATHEND⟧ — 若目标是反向 KL，则它对应的是正向 KL 梯度 ✗✗
⟦INLMATH98MATHEND⟧ 梯度正确（反向 KL），低方差，数值无偏 需显式构造 ⟦INLMATH99MATHEND⟧，实现稍复杂 ✓

注：⟦INLMATH100MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH101MATHEND⟧ 的梯度完全相同（样本级等价）。On-policy 时推荐直接用 ⟦INLMATH102MATHEND⟧，实现最简单。

2.2 Off-policy 下把反向 KL 写成损失项

损失项写法 优点 问题 推荐
⟦INLMATH103MATHEND⟧ 梯度正确（反向 KL），数值无偏 方差较高 △
⟦INLMATH104MATHEND⟧ — 梯度对应局部二阶 surrogate（非反向 KL），与本文目标不匹配 ✗✗
⟦INLMATH105MATHEND⟧ 梯度正确（反向 KL），低方差 数值有偏（小 KL 邻域通常偏差较小） ✓✓
⟦INLMATH106MATHEND⟧ 梯度正确（反向 KL），低方差，数值无偏 — ✓✓

注：⟦INLMATH107MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH108MATHEND⟧ 的梯度完全相同（样本级等价）。两者均为推荐选择。

2.3 把 KL 写成奖励塑形项（stop-grad shaped reward）

估计器 优点 问题 推荐
⟦INLMATH109MATHEND⟧ 数值无偏，诱导的梯度主项无偏 方差较高 ✓✓
⟦INLMATH110MATHEND⟧ 数值有偏 诱导的策略梯度一般有偏（通常不等于目标项） ✗✗
⟦INLMATH111MATHEND⟧ 数值无偏，低方差 诱导的策略梯度有偏，偏差项为 ⟦INLMATH112MATHEND⟧，实践中可能显著不稳定甚至崩溃 ✗✗

注：在本文讨论的 stop-grad reward shaping 场景下，只有 ⟦INLMATH113MATHEND⟧ 能保持与反向 KL 正则一致的无偏策略梯度主项。⟦INLMATH114MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH115MATHEND⟧ 都会引入偏差；其中 ⟦INLMATH116MATHEND⟧ 虽然数值无偏且方差低，但实践中曾观察到显著不稳定甚至崩溃。

图例：✓✓ 强烈推荐；✓ 推荐（实现稍复杂或有小缺点）；△ 可用需谨慎；✗✗ 与目标不匹配。

想看推导、方差分析、常见陷阱，直接往下读。

3. 准备工作：符号与基本概念

在进入正文前，先统一符号，并把后文反复要用的两个基础结论写清楚。

3.1 符号、采样分布与解析梯度

符号约定

  • ⟦INLMATH117MATHEND⟧：当前 actor 策略（参数为 ⟦INLMATH118MATHEND⟧）
  • ⟦INLMATH119MATHEND⟧：若无歧义，后文简写 ⟦INLMATH120MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH121MATHEND⟧：参考策略（reference policy），不依赖于 ⟦INLMATH122MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH123MATHEND⟧：行为策略（behavior policy），用于 off-policy 采样，不依赖于 ⟦INLMATH124MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH125MATHEND⟧：score function（得分函数）
  • ⟦INLMATH126MATHEND⟧：stop-gradient 操作（在代码中对应 .detach()）

统一的采样策略视角：引入 ⟦INLMATH127MATHEND⟧ 记号

分析 KL 估计器的梯度性质时，on-policy 和 off-policy 看起来像要分开处理，但其实可以放进同一个框架。

为此引入采样策略 ⟦INLMATH128MATHEND⟧，即数据来自分布 ⟦INLMATH129MATHEND⟧，并定义统一的重要性权重比率：

⟦DISPMATH3MATHEND⟧

这个定义的关键是：无论 on-policy 还是 off-policy，都把采样策略 ⟦INLMATH130MATHEND⟧ 视为梯度常数（即对 ⟦INLMATH131MATHEND⟧ 应用 stop-gradient）。

  • Off-policy（⟦INLMATH132MATHEND⟧）：由于 ⟦INLMATH133MATHEND⟧ 本身不依赖于 ⟦INLMATH134MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH135MATHEND⟧，此时 ⟦INLMATH136MATHEND⟧。
  • On-policy（⟦INLMATH137MATHEND⟧）：令 ⟦INLMATH138MATHEND⟧ 但对其应用 stop-gradient，则 ⟦INLMATH139MATHEND⟧（数值恒为 1），但 ⟦INLMATH140MATHEND⟧。

在 on-policy 情形下，虽然数值上 ⟦INLMATH141MATHEND⟧，仍要在计算图中显式构造 ⟦INLMATH142MATHEND⟧（或等价写成 ⟦INLMATH143MATHEND⟧）。如果直接把 ⟦INLMATH144MATHEND⟧ 写成常数 1，score function 这条梯度路径就会丢掉，推导也会退化成后文所说的”朴素 on-policy 实现”。

⟦INLMATH145MATHEND⟧ 补上的正是”采样分布对参数 ⟦INLMATH146MATHEND⟧ 的依赖”这条梯度路径。On-policy 时，”先取期望后求梯度”和”先求梯度后取期望”之所以结果不同，就是因为少了这条路径；显式构造 ⟦INLMATH147MATHEND⟧，就是把它补回来。

有了这个记号，后面的推导就不必再分 on-policy 和 off-policy 两套写法。

得分函数与 KL 散度的解析梯度

得分函数有一个重要性质：⟦INLMATH148MATHEND⟧（由 ⟦INLMATH149MATHEND⟧ 可得）。

基于这一性质，可以推导出正向与反向 KL 散度关于参数 ⟦INLMATH150MATHEND⟧ 的解析梯度。

反向 KL 的梯度：

⟦DISPMATH4MATHEND⟧

对 ⟦INLMATH151MATHEND⟧ 求梯度（应用乘积法则）：

⟦DISPMATH5MATHEND⟧

利用 ⟦INLMATH152MATHEND⟧，以及 ⟦INLMATH153MATHEND⟧、⟦INLMATH154MATHEND⟧，可得：

⟦DISPMATH6MATHEND⟧

即：

⟦DISPMATH7MATHEND⟧

注：后文将定义 ⟦INLMATH155MATHEND⟧，因此上式可简写为 ⟦INLMATH156MATHEND⟧，这一形式在后续梯度分析中会反复出现。

正向 KL 散度的梯度：

⟦DISPMATH8MATHEND⟧

由于 ⟦INLMATH157MATHEND⟧ 不依赖于参数 ⟦INLMATH158MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH9MATHEND⟧

为了用来自 ⟦INLMATH159MATHEND⟧ 的样本估计该梯度，引入重要性采样：

⟦DISPMATH10MATHEND⟧

其中最后一步用到了 ⟦INLMATH160MATHEND⟧。因此

⟦DISPMATH11MATHEND⟧

注：后文将推导 ⟦INLMATH161MATHEND⟧，因此 ⟦INLMATH162MATHEND⟧（正向 KL）——这解释了为何直接对 ⟦INLMATH163MATHEND⟧ 反向传播会给出”错误”的梯度方向。

有了这两个结果，后续就能判断各个估计器的梯度期望各自对应哪种 KL 散度的解析梯度。

4. 三种估计器的定义与设计原理

围绕概率比值 ⟦INLMATH164MATHEND⟧，John Schulman 在一篇经典笔记里系统比较了三种单样本 KL 估计器。本节先把它们的定义和设计动机放在一起看。

4.1 三种估计器：定义与直观解释

⟦INLMATH165MATHEND⟧：最朴素的 log-ratio 估计器

⟦DISPMATH12MATHEND⟧

最直观的定义——直接取对数比值的负值。它对反向 KL 无偏，主要问题不是目标错了，而是单样本值可正可负；当真实 KL 很小时，样本仍可能大幅正负摆动，所以相对方差往往很高。

⟦INLMATH166MATHEND⟧：基于平方 log-ratio 的估计器

⟦DISPMATH13MATHEND⟧

设计动机：⟦INLMATH167MATHEND⟧ 的估计值可正可负，⟦INLMATH168MATHEND⟧ 通过取平方让每个样本的估计值都非负，这样每个样本都能直观地衡量 ⟦INLMATH169MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH170MATHEND⟧ 之间的差异。

为什么偏差通常不大？ 更准确地说，⟦INLMATH171MATHEND⟧ 不是标准 KL，但在 ⟦INLMATH172MATHEND⟧ 的邻域里它和反向 KL 共享相同的二阶局部展开，因此可以看作一个局部有效的 surrogate；一旦离开小 KL 邻域，这个近似就未必还可靠。

技术注记：⟦INLMATH173MATHEND⟧ 为什么与反向 KL 具有相同的二阶局部行为？</summary>

若取 ⟦INLMATH174MATHEND⟧ 使得 ⟦INLMATH175MATHEND⟧，并在标准正则性条件下（以保证积分与微分可交换）对参数做小扰动 ⟦INLMATH176MATHEND⟧，则有

⟦DISPMATH14MATHEND⟧

同时

⟦DISPMATH15MATHEND⟧

其中 ⟦INLMATH177MATHEND⟧ 是 ⟦INLMATH178MATHEND⟧ 处的 Fisher 信息矩阵。

</details>

⟦INLMATH179MATHEND⟧：控制变量法构造的 Bregman 散度估计器

⟦DISPMATH16MATHEND⟧

设计动机：希望得到一个既无偏又低方差的估计器。标准做法是给 ⟦INLMATH180MATHEND⟧ 加一个控制变量（control variate）——即期望为零、但与 ⟦INLMATH181MATHEND⟧ 负相关的量。

注意到 ⟦INLMATH182MATHEND⟧，因此对任意 ⟦INLMATH183MATHEND⟧，

⟦DISPMATH17MATHEND⟧

仍是无偏估计。

为什么选 ⟦INLMATH184MATHEND⟧？ 由于 ⟦INLMATH185MATHEND⟧ 是凹函数，⟦INLMATH186MATHEND⟧，所以

⟦DISPMATH18MATHEND⟧

恒非负！每个样本都”正向”贡献信息，避免了 ⟦INLMATH187MATHEND⟧ 中正负估计值相互抵消的问题。

几何视角：⟦INLMATH188MATHEND⟧ 其实就是一个 Bregman 散度。考虑凸函数 ⟦INLMATH189MATHEND⟧，它在 ⟦INLMATH190MATHEND⟧ 处的切线为 ⟦INLMATH191MATHEND⟧。Bregman 散度定义为函数值与切线值之差：

⟦DISPMATH19MATHEND⟧

凸函数始终位于其切线上方，所以这个差值自然非负。更关键的是，当 ⟦INLMATH192MATHEND⟧ 时，函数与切线越来越贴近，差值以 ⟦INLMATH193MATHEND⟧ 的二阶速度趋近于零——这正是 ⟦INLMATH194MATHEND⟧ 在两策略接近时方差较小的根本原因。

三者的设计逻辑对比如下：

估计器 定义 设计原理
⟦INLMATH195MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH196MATHEND⟧ 最朴素定义
⟦INLMATH197MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH198MATHEND⟧ 局部二阶行为与 KL 一致的 surrogate
⟦INLMATH199MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH200MATHEND⟧ 控制变量 + Bregman 散度

5. 数值估计：偏差与方差

假设从 ⟦INLMATH201MATHEND⟧ 采样来估计反向 KL ⟦INLMATH202MATHEND⟧：

5.1 无偏性分析

⟦DISPMATH20MATHEND⟧

估计反向 KL 的数值时，⟦INLMATH203MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH204MATHEND⟧ 无偏，⟦INLMATH205MATHEND⟧ 有偏。

5.2 方差特性分析

John Schulman 的实验（⟦INLMATH206MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH207MATHEND⟧，真实 KL = 0.005）显示：

估计器 偏差/真值 标准差/真值
⟦INLMATH208MATHEND⟧ 0 20
⟦INLMATH209MATHEND⟧ 0.002 1.42
⟦INLMATH210MATHEND⟧ 0 1.42

当 KL 较大时（⟦INLMATH211MATHEND⟧，真实 KL = 0.5）：

估计器 偏差/真值 标准差/真值
⟦INLMATH212MATHEND⟧ 0 2
⟦INLMATH213MATHEND⟧ 0.25 1.73
⟦INLMATH214MATHEND⟧ 0 1.7

直觉上：

  • ⟦INLMATH215MATHEND⟧ 以一阶项起步，当 ⟦INLMATH216MATHEND⟧ 接近 1 时波动较大，且可能取负值；
  • ⟦INLMATH217MATHEND⟧ 在 ⟦INLMATH218MATHEND⟧ 处是二阶小量，始终非负，所以两策略接近时方差较小；
  • 但离开小 KL、覆盖良好的局部区域后（⟦INLMATH219MATHEND⟧ 可能极大），⟦INLMATH220MATHEND⟧ 的方差也会因权重爆炸而迅速上升；此时 ⟦INLMATH221MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH222MATHEND⟧ 的优劣就不能简单一刀切了。

从纯数值估计的角度看，可以先记下面这张表：

估计器 对数值的偏差 方差特性
⟦INLMATH223MATHEND⟧ 无偏 高（可正可负）
⟦INLMATH224MATHEND⟧ 有偏（小 KL 邻域通常较小） 低（恒正）
⟦INLMATH225MATHEND⟧ 无偏 低（恒正）

如果只看 KL 数值本身，那么在 小 KL、覆盖良好 的常见局部场景下，⟦INLMATH226MATHEND⟧ 往往是更稳妥的选择。

注：若要估计正向 KL 的数值 ⟦INLMATH227MATHEND⟧，而只能从 ⟦INLMATH228MATHEND⟧ 采样，则可以用重要性采样 ⟦INLMATH229MATHEND⟧。

6. KL 惩罚的两种使用方式

接下来真正分出岔路的是 KL 惩罚在实现里到底怎么用。这一步决定了只关心估计器的数值性质，还是要把梯度性质一并算进去。

回顾 KL 正则化强化学习的目标函数（下式中 ⟦INLMATH230MATHEND⟧ 表示”由策略 ⟦INLMATH231MATHEND⟧ 诱导的轨迹分布”）：

⟦DISPMATH21MATHEND⟧

这个数学形式看似统一，但在基于策略梯度（Policy Gradient）的算法（如 PPO）中实现时，会分化出两种截然不同的范式——代码上可能只差几行，优化语义却完全不同。

符号说明：本节用 ⟦INLMATH232MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH233MATHEND⟧ 泛指某个 token/state 级的 KL 估计器（如 ⟦INLMATH234MATHEND⟧），具体定义见前文”三种估计器的定义与设计原理”。

6.1 作为损失项：KL 直接反传

actor_loss = -advantage * log_prob + beta * kl  # kl 参与梯度计算

Critic 只学习环境价值，KL 作为 actor 的正则项直接参与 loss 的反向传播。

6.2 作为奖励塑形项：KL 只改 reward，不直接反传

kl = compute_kl(log_prob_q, log_prob_p).detach()
shaped_reward = reward - beta * kl

KL 被视为环境奖励的一部分，用塑形后的奖励进行标准的 actor-critic 更新。KL 项本身不参与 loss 的反向传播。

很多实现里这两种写法只差一个 .detach()；但从优化语义看，它们并不是同一种算法。差别先说清楚：

  • KL 作为损失项：需要 KL 估计器给出正确的显式梯度，关心梯度对应哪个优化目标；
  • KL 作为奖励塑形项：需要 KL 的数值估计准确，同时还要看它诱导的策略梯度是否正确。

7. 作为损失项时的梯度分析

当 KL 散度作为损失函数参与反向传播时，不同估计器对应的优化目标并不相同。这里也是实践中最容易踩坑的地方。

下面沿用前文的统一框架，把 on-policy 与 off-policy 放进同一套推导。回顾比率定义：

⟦DISPMATH22MATHEND⟧

其中 ⟦INLMATH235MATHEND⟧ 为采样策略。在此框架下：

  • On-policy（⟦INLMATH236MATHEND⟧）：⟦INLMATH237MATHEND⟧，但 ⟦INLMATH238MATHEND⟧
  • Off-policy（⟦INLMATH239MATHEND⟧）：⟦INLMATH240MATHEND⟧，且 ⟦INLMATH241MATHEND⟧

7.1 三种估计器的基本梯度

先计算三种估计器本身的梯度（不含 ⟦INLMATH242MATHEND⟧），这些结果会在后续分析中反复使用。

推导 ⟦INLMATH243MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH23MATHEND⟧

⟦DISPMATH24MATHEND⟧

推导 ⟦INLMATH244MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH25MATHEND⟧

由链式法则：

⟦DISPMATH26MATHEND⟧

推导 ⟦INLMATH245MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH27MATHEND⟧

首先计算 ⟦INLMATH246MATHEND⟧。由于 ⟦INLMATH247MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH28MATHEND⟧

再计算 ⟦INLMATH248MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH29MATHEND⟧

因此：

⟦DISPMATH30MATHEND⟧

三种估计器的梯度分别为：

  • ⟦INLMATH249MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH250MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH251MATHEND⟧

这些基本梯度会在后续的统一框架分析中反复出现。

“先期望后梯度” vs “先梯度后期望”：一个重要警示

分析 KL 估计器的梯度时，有一个容易混淆的陷阱：“先期望后梯度”和”先梯度后期望”可能给出不同结果。

如果从解析角度把 ⟦INLMATH252MATHEND⟧ 看成 ⟦INLMATH253MATHEND⟧ 的函数再求梯度（即”先期望后梯度”），由”数值估计”一节的结论 ⟦INLMATH254MATHEND⟧，可得：

⟦DISPMATH31MATHEND⟧

⟦DISPMATH32MATHEND⟧

两者都给出反向 KL 的梯度。但在代码中直接对 ⟦INLMATH255MATHEND⟧ 的样本均值反向传播时，自动微分执行的是”先梯度后期望”，得到的是 ⟦INLMATH256MATHEND⟧——这与”先期望后梯度”的结果可能不同。

差异的根源在于：当采样分布 ⟦INLMATH257MATHEND⟧ 本身依赖于 ⟦INLMATH258MATHEND⟧ 时，期望与梯度不能随意交换。这正是 on-policy 场景最麻烦的地方，也是为什么需要把 ⟦INLMATH259MATHEND⟧ 这条路径显式写出来。

7.2 统一框架下的梯度分析

下面用 ⟦INLMATH260MATHEND⟧ 框架统一处理 on-policy 和 off-policy。考虑损失函数形式 ⟦INLMATH261MATHEND⟧，其中 ⟦INLMATH262MATHEND⟧。

以下期望一律指对固定采样分布 ⟦INLMATH263MATHEND⟧ 的 ⟦INLMATH264MATHEND⟧。既然 ⟦INLMATH265MATHEND⟧ 不依赖于 ⟦INLMATH266MATHEND⟧，对任何关于 ⟦INLMATH267MATHEND⟧ 可微的函数 ⟦INLMATH268MATHEND⟧，都有

⟦DISPMATH33MATHEND⟧

也就是说，在 ⟦INLMATH269MATHEND⟧ 框架下，”先期望后梯度”和”先梯度后期望”对 ⟦INLMATH270MATHEND⟧ 是等价的；但这并不意味着对 ⟦INLMATH271MATHEND⟧ 也能无条件交换微分与期望。

统一框架下三种估计器的梯度推导

利用 ⟦INLMATH272MATHEND⟧（因为 ⟦INLMATH273MATHEND⟧），结合前文推导的 ⟦INLMATH274MATHEND⟧，应用乘积法则：

⟦INLMATH275MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH34MATHEND⟧

⟦INLMATH276MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH35MATHEND⟧

⟦INLMATH277MATHEND⟧（对 ⟦INLMATH278MATHEND⟧ 施加 stop-gradient）：

⟦DISPMATH36MATHEND⟧

⟦INLMATH279MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH37MATHEND⟧

代入 ⟦INLMATH280MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH38MATHEND⟧

这里有个很关键的消去：

⟦DISPMATH39MATHEND⟧

梯度期望与优化目标

利用 ⟦INLMATH281MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH282MATHEND⟧：

⟦INLMATH283MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH40MATHEND⟧

⟦INLMATH284MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH41MATHEND⟧

也就是说，⟦INLMATH285MATHEND⟧ 的梯度期望对应的是”最小化 ⟦INLMATH286MATHEND⟧”（一个与 KL 共享局部二阶行为的 surrogate），而不是反向 KL ⟦INLMATH287MATHEND⟧ 的解析梯度。因此若目标是精确优化反向 KL，不建议直接使用 ⟦INLMATH288MATHEND⟧。

⟦INLMATH289MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH42MATHEND⟧

⟦INLMATH290MATHEND⟧：

⟦DISPMATH43MATHEND⟧

梯度等价性：哪些方法产生相同的梯度随机变量

这一步也解释了为什么 ⟦INLMATH291MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH292MATHEND⟧ 常一起出现：对同一个样本 ⟦INLMATH293MATHEND⟧，它们反传得到的梯度向量其实完全一样，都是 ⟦INLMATH294MATHEND⟧。不只是期望相同，而是样本级一致；因此均值、方差和更高阶统计量也都一致。

损失项写法 梯度随机变量 梯度期望 对应的优化目标
⟦INLMATH295MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH296MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH297MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓
⟦INLMATH298MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH299MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH300MATHEND⟧ 局部二阶 surrogate（非反向 KL）✗
⟦INLMATH301MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH302MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH303MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓
⟦INLMATH304MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH305MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH306MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓

7.3 On-policy 与 Off-policy 的统一视角

有了上面这一步，再回头看 on-policy 和 off-policy 的关系就清楚多了。

On-policy（⟦INLMATH307MATHEND⟧）：

  • ⟦INLMATH308MATHEND⟧（数值恒为 1）；
  • ⟦INLMATH309MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH310MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH311MATHEND⟧；
  • 但梯度不同！因为 ⟦INLMATH312MATHEND⟧。

这解释了为什么 on-policy 下朴素直接反向传播（不显式构造 ⟦INLMATH313MATHEND⟧）使用 ⟦INLMATH314MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH315MATHEND⟧ 作为损失会出问题：

  • 直接用 ⟦INLMATH316MATHEND⟧：相当于没有 ⟦INLMATH317MATHEND⟧ 的版本，⟦INLMATH318MATHEND⟧，完全无效；
  • 直接用 ⟦INLMATH319MATHEND⟧：相当于没有 ⟦INLMATH320MATHEND⟧ 的版本，⟦INLMATH321MATHEND⟧（正向 KL），方向错误；
  • 直接用 ⟦INLMATH322MATHEND⟧：⟦INLMATH323MATHEND⟧（反向 KL）✓ 在这种朴素实现下是与目标一致的选择。

但若显式构造 ⟦INLMATH324MATHEND⟧，则：

  • 可用：⟦INLMATH325MATHEND⟧（方差高）、⟦INLMATH326MATHEND⟧（推荐）、⟦INLMATH327MATHEND⟧（推荐）——三者均给出反向 KL 梯度；
  • 不宜直接用于精确优化反向 KL：⟦INLMATH328MATHEND⟧（⟦INLMATH329MATHEND⟧ 参与梯度）——优化的是与 KL 局部二阶行为一致的 surrogate，而非反向 KL。

Off-policy（⟦INLMATH330MATHEND⟧）：

  • ⟦INLMATH331MATHEND⟧（标准重要性权重）；
  • 可用：⟦INLMATH332MATHEND⟧（方差高）、⟦INLMATH333MATHEND⟧（推荐）、⟦INLMATH334MATHEND⟧（推荐）——三者均给出反向 KL 梯度；
  • 不宜直接用于精确优化反向 KL：⟦INLMATH335MATHEND⟧（⟦INLMATH336MATHEND⟧ 参与梯度）——优化的是与 KL 局部二阶行为一致的 surrogate，而非反向 KL。

on-policy 下 ⟦INLMATH337MATHEND⟧ 能直接奏效并不是一个可以外推的普遍现象，而是 ⟦INLMATH338MATHEND⟧ 时的一种特殊退化：此时 ⟦INLMATH339MATHEND⟧，恰好落在正确的反向 KL 梯度上；这个结论不能直接外推到一般 off-policy 情形。

关于大模型 off-policy 场景的更深入分析，可参见我之前的博客：从两策略到三策略：LLM RL 中行为策略–参考策略不一致下的 TRPO 扩展。

7.4 方差分析

前面看到，给出反向 KL 无偏梯度的有三个选择：⟦INLMATH340MATHEND⟧、⟦INLMATH341MATHEND⟧、⟦INLMATH342MATHEND⟧。它们的梯度随机变量分别为（注意 ⟦INLMATH343MATHEND⟧ 是向量，因此梯度也是向量）：

⟦DISPMATH44MATHEND⟧

其中 ⟦INLMATH344MATHEND⟧ 对应 ⟦INLMATH345MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH346MATHEND⟧（两者完全相同）。

为了避免”向量梯度的方差”这一表述的歧义，这里比较任意方向上的投影方差：取任意单位向量 ⟦INLMATH347MATHEND⟧，定义标量随机变量

⟦DISPMATH45MATHEND⟧

令 ⟦INLMATH348MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH349MATHEND⟧，则

⟦DISPMATH46MATHEND⟧

两者期望相同，且任意方向上的方差之差为

⟦DISPMATH47MATHEND⟧

在典型的 KL 惩罚场景下（⟦INLMATH350MATHEND⟧），取 ⟦INLMATH351MATHEND⟧，⟦INLMATH352MATHEND⟧：

  • ⟦INLMATH353MATHEND⟧
  • ⟦INLMATH354MATHEND⟧，主导项为正的 ⟦INLMATH355MATHEND⟧ 常数

因此在足够小的邻域里，⟦INLMATH356MATHEND⟧。

一旦离开这个小 KL 邻域，⟦INLMATH357MATHEND⟧ 的符号就不再固定；此时整体比较还要结合 ⟦INLMATH358MATHEND⟧ 与 score 项的加权来判断，不能再简单依赖这一局部展开。

直觉上：

  • ⟦INLMATH359MATHEND⟧ 包含一个量级为 ⟦INLMATH360MATHEND⟧ 的零均值噪声项 ⟦INLMATH361MATHEND⟧；
  • ⟦INLMATH362MATHEND⟧ 已把这个常数噪声项消去，只剩下与 ⟦INLMATH363MATHEND⟧ 成正比的一阶小量。

方差对比如下：

估计器 梯度随机变量 系数量级（⟦INLMATH364MATHEND⟧） 方差
⟦INLMATH365MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH366MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH367MATHEND⟧ 高
⟦INLMATH368MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH369MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH370MATHEND⟧ 低
⟦INLMATH371MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH372MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH373MATHEND⟧ 低

⟦INLMATH374MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH375MATHEND⟧ 给出的是同一个梯度随机变量；相比之下，⟦INLMATH376MATHEND⟧ 多出一个零均值的常数噪声项，所以在典型的小 KL 场景里通常更吵。

实践建议：若优化反向 KL，首选 ⟦INLMATH377MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH378MATHEND⟧（两者梯度等价且方差低）；⟦INLMATH379MATHEND⟧ 虽然无偏但方差较高，可作备选，并需配合 clipping / 正则化。

极度 off-policy 时的警示：

当 ⟦INLMATH380MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH381MATHEND⟧ 差异很大时——例如 ⟦INLMATH382MATHEND⟧ 在 ⟦INLMATH383MATHEND⟧ 的高密度区域几乎没有采样，或 ⟦INLMATH384MATHEND⟧ 在尾部爆炸——任何基于 ⟦INLMATH385MATHEND⟧ 的方法都会遇到严重的方差问题。此时 ⟦INLMATH386MATHEND⟧（或 ⟦INLMATH387MATHEND⟧）相对于 ⟦INLMATH388MATHEND⟧ 的优势就不再有理论保证，需要结合 clipping、正则化等手段综合处理。

不过在 RL 实践中，通常会控制 KL 约束、限制 off-policy 程度（例如使用近邻策略 ⟦INLMATH389MATHEND⟧）。在这种常见场景下，可以比较有信心地说：

一旦决定用重要性采样优化反向 KL，推荐使用 ⟦INLMATH390MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH391MATHEND⟧（两者梯度等价且方差低）；相比之下，⟦INLMATH392MATHEND⟧ 方差更高。

与本文的分析一致，DeepSeek-V3.2 技术报告采用 ⟦INLMATH393MATHEND⟧ 作为 off-policy KL 惩罚的估计器。在本文记号下，这正是 ⟦INLMATH394MATHEND⟧：用当前策略与行为策略的比率修正 ⟦INLMATH395MATHEND⟧，从而同时恢复无偏 KL 数值估计和无偏梯度。

梯度分析总览表

统一框架下各估计器对应的梯度目标如下：

采样类型 Loss ⟦INLMATH396MATHEND⟧ Loss 的期望 对应的优化目标 能否用于优化反向 KL？
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH397MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH398MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓（但方差较高）
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH399MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH400MATHEND⟧ 局部二阶 surrogate（非反向 KL） ✗
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH401MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH402MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓（推荐，低方差）
on/off-policy ⟦INLMATH403MATHEND⟧ ⟦INLMATH404MATHEND⟧ 反向 KL ✓（推荐，低方差）

其中 ⟦INLMATH405MATHEND⟧。当 on-policy（⟦INLMATH406MATHEND⟧）时，⟦INLMATH407MATHEND⟧。

这里要特别强调：上表的结论针对的是”loss 写成 ⟦INLMATH408MATHEND⟧ 且 ⟦INLMATH409MATHEND⟧ 在计算图中保留梯度路径”的统一框架。on-policy 时虽然数值上 ⟦INLMATH410MATHEND⟧，但由于 ⟦INLMATH411MATHEND⟧，仍有 ⟦INLMATH412MATHEND⟧，因此 ⟦INLMATH413MATHEND⟧ 与”直接对 ⟦INLMATH414MATHEND⟧ 的样本均值反向传播”在梯度上并不等价。

如果采用朴素 on-policy 写法（即从 ⟦INLMATH415MATHEND⟧ 采样后把 ⟦INLMATH416MATHEND⟧ 视为普通标量，直接对其样本均值反向传播，不显式构造 ⟦INLMATH417MATHEND⟧ 来补上 score-function 路径），则退化为：

  • 直接使用 ⟦INLMATH418MATHEND⟧：⟦INLMATH419MATHEND⟧（无效）；
  • 直接使用 ⟦INLMATH420MATHEND⟧：⟦INLMATH421MATHEND⟧（反向 KL）✓；
  • 直接使用 ⟦INLMATH422MATHEND⟧：⟦INLMATH423MATHEND⟧（正向 KL）✗。

把上面的结果压成一句话：

  1. On-policy 优化反向 KL（朴素直接反向传播）：在本文限定的实现方式下，⟦INLMATH424MATHEND⟧ 是与目标最一致的选择。
  2. Off-policy 优化反向 KL：有三个正确选项：
    • ⟦INLMATH425MATHEND⟧：无偏但方差较高；
    • ⟦INLMATH426MATHEND⟧：无偏，与 ⟦INLMATH427MATHEND⟧ 梯度完全等价；
    • ⟦INLMATH428MATHEND⟧：无偏且方差更低（与上一项等价，均为推荐选择）。
  3. ⟦INLMATH429MATHEND⟧（权重参与梯度）不对应本文目标：它优化的是一个只在局部二阶行为上与 KL 一致的 surrogate，而不是反向 KL；这是一个容易被忽视的陷阱。

8. 作为奖励塑形项时的梯度分析

这里是最容易掉的坑：既然 ⟦INLMATH430MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH431MATHEND⟧ 对反向 KL 的数值估计都无偏，那把它们加上 stop-gradient 放进奖励塑形里，是不是也应该没问题？

答案是否定的。数值无偏不推出放进 reward 后梯度仍然正确。因为一旦 KL 变成 shaped reward 的一部分，真正进入优化的是 ⟦INLMATH432MATHEND⟧，而不是 ⟦INLMATH433MATHEND⟧ 本身。

8.1 真正的 KL 正则化策略梯度

考虑 KL 正则化的强化学习目标：

⟦DISPMATH48MATHEND⟧

其解析梯度为：

⟦DISPMATH49MATHEND⟧

利用前文「准备工作」章节的结论，反向 KL 的梯度为：

⟦DISPMATH50MATHEND⟧

因此，真正的 KL 正则化策略梯度是：

⟦DISPMATH51MATHEND⟧

使用估计器 ⟦INLMATH434MATHEND⟧ 时的梯度形式

下文只讨论策略梯度主项本身，暂不把 baseline、critic 拟合误差、GAE 与额外归一化一并纳入。此时若把某个估计器 ⟦INLMATH435MATHEND⟧（加 stop-gradient）放进奖励塑形，塑形后的奖励为 ⟦INLMATH436MATHEND⟧，策略梯度变为：

⟦DISPMATH52MATHEND⟧

在该主项分析下，无偏条件是：⟦INLMATH437MATHEND⟧ 当且仅当

⟦DISPMATH53MATHEND⟧

使用 ⟦INLMATH438MATHEND⟧ 作为惩罚：梯度无偏

当 ⟦INLMATH439MATHEND⟧ 时，条件自动成立：

⟦DISPMATH54MATHEND⟧

所以就该策略梯度主项而言，把 ⟦INLMATH440MATHEND⟧ 放进奖励塑形诱导的是无偏梯度。

使用 ⟦INLMATH441MATHEND⟧ 作为惩罚：梯度有偏

当 ⟦INLMATH442MATHEND⟧ 时：

⟦DISPMATH55MATHEND⟧

第二项正是 ⟦INLMATH443MATHEND⟧。问题出在第一项：

⟦DISPMATH56MATHEND⟧

而这个量可以改写为：

⟦DISPMATH57MATHEND⟧

利用正向 KL 的梯度公式 ⟦INLMATH444MATHEND⟧，有：

⟦DISPMATH58MATHEND⟧

因此：

⟦DISPMATH59MATHEND⟧

把 ⟦INLMATH445MATHEND⟧ 放进奖励塑形时，梯度是有偏的，偏差项正好等于正向 KL 梯度的负值。

更严格地讲，把 ⟦INLMATH446MATHEND⟧ 放进奖励塑形时，实际更新会在反向 KL 梯度之外额外混入一个与正向 KL 梯度相关的偏差项，因此不再对应纯粹的反向 KL 正则目标。这也是它在实践中容易变得不稳定的原因。

实验现象：Shah et al. (2025) 的实验表明，在其 on-policy LLM-RL 设定下：

  • ⟦INLMATH447MATHEND⟧ in reward：训练稳定；
  • ⟦INLMATH448MATHEND⟧ in reward：出现显著不稳定，甚至训练崩溃。

这与本文给出的偏差分析方向一致。

使用 ⟦INLMATH449MATHEND⟧ 作为惩罚：同样有偏

当 ⟦INLMATH450MATHEND⟧ 时，奖励塑形项对应的梯度项为

⟦DISPMATH60MATHEND⟧

它一般不等于 ⟦INLMATH451MATHEND⟧。所以把 ⟦INLMATH452MATHEND⟧ 放进奖励塑形时同样会诱导出有偏的策略梯度。

Off-policy 场景下的结论

上述分析假设 on-policy 采样。换到 off-policy 场景，结论会变吗？

设样本来自行为策略 ⟦INLMATH453MATHEND⟧，采用重要性加权的策略梯度：

⟦DISPMATH61MATHEND⟧

利用 ⟦INLMATH454MATHEND⟧，上式等于：

⟦DISPMATH62MATHEND⟧

无偏条件仍然是 ⟦INLMATH455MATHEND⟧，与 on-policy 完全一致。

这里要单独强调一点：在本文关注的当前样本 / 当前 token 级 off-policy 策略梯度主项里，重要性权重 ⟦INLMATH456MATHEND⟧ 作用在整个策略梯度估计器上，不需要再对 shaped reward 中的 KL 估计器额外乘一层权重。因此：

  • Shaped reward 保持原形式：⟦INLMATH457MATHEND⟧（而不是 ⟦INLMATH458MATHEND⟧）；
  • 在本文讨论的 stop-grad reward shaping（⟦INLMATH459MATHEND⟧）、目标为 反向 KL 正则 的设定下：结论和 on-policy 的策略梯度主项相同，只有 ⟦INLMATH460MATHEND⟧ 能保证梯度主项无偏。

注：这里默认的是当前样本 / 当前 token 级的 reward shaping 写法；若回到一般多步 MDP 的严格 off-policy 推导，还需要配合逐步重要性采样或相应的值函数修正。

8.2 这一节的结论：只有 ⟦INLMATH461MATHEND⟧ 保持无偏

估计器 数值无偏？ 作为奖励塑形项时梯度主项无偏？ 实际表现
⟦INLMATH462MATHEND⟧ ✓ ✓ 稳定
⟦INLMATH463MATHEND⟧ ✗ ✗ 不建议
⟦INLMATH464MATHEND⟧ ✓ ✗ 显著不稳定

回头看，评价 KL 估计器时，”数值无偏”和”梯度正确”其实是两个独立维度。就本文讨论的奖励塑形写法（stop-grad reward shaping，目标是反向 KL 正则；无论 on-policy 还是 off-policy）而言，就策略梯度主项来说，只有 ⟦INLMATH465MATHEND⟧ 满足目标要求。⟦INLMATH466MATHEND⟧ 虽然数值无偏且方差更低，但放进奖励塑形后会导致梯度有偏，实践里也确实更容易不稳定。

补充说明：一旦把 learned critic、GAE、baseline 归一化等实现细节都考虑进来，实际更新中的偏差分析会复杂得多。本节结论刻意只聚焦在 policy-gradient 主项，就是为了避免把不同来源的偏差混在一起。

到这里容易产生一个”表面矛盾”：

  • 在 奖励塑形项 里强调的是”若目标是本文讨论的 reverse-KL stop-grad shaping，则只有 ⟦INLMATH467MATHEND⟧ 在策略梯度主项上无偏”；
  • 但在前文 损失项反传（尤其是 off-policy）里，又推荐用 ⟦INLMATH468MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH469MATHEND⟧ 来获得更低方差的反向 KL 梯度。

下面就来解释两者并不冲突：就”KL 正则项对应的 policy-gradient 随机变量”而言，它们甚至可以做到样本级完全等价；差异主要来自 KL 是否进入 advantage / baseline、以及信用分配（credit assignment）的路径。

8.3 ⟦INLMATH470MATHEND⟧ 奖励塑形与低方差 KL 损失项：等价性与差异

说到这里，很容易追问一句：KL 写进 loss 和 KL 写进 reward，到底在什么意义上等价，又在什么意义上不是一回事？

KL 梯度项的样本级等价性

这里说的”等价”只限于 KL 正则对应的那一项梯度随机变量；一旦把 learned critic、baseline、GAE、batch 中心化一并算进来，整体更新语义就会分叉。本节统一写成 policy gradient 的上升方向 ⟦INLMATH471MATHEND⟧（若代码里是最小化 loss，则整体只差一个全局负号，不影响等价性结论），并沿用前文的统一权重记号：样本来自 ⟦INLMATH472MATHEND⟧，重要性权重 ⟦INLMATH473MATHEND⟧ 作用在策略梯度估计器上。

KL 作为损失项（低方差选择）：前文已证明，采用 ⟦INLMATH474MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH475MATHEND⟧ 作为正则项时，梯度随机变量都化简为

⟦DISPMATH63MATHEND⟧

KL 作为奖励塑形项（⟦INLMATH476MATHEND⟧ in reward）：shaped reward 为 ⟦INLMATH477MATHEND⟧（对 ⟦INLMATH478MATHEND⟧ 做 stop-gradient 只是在实现上避免”KL 直接反传”，不改变它作为惩罚的数值）。在”策略梯度项”里，KL 惩罚贡献的是

⟦DISPMATH64MATHEND⟧

这也解释了为什么前面 8.1 / 8.2 节和第 7 章（损失项）看起来像在推荐不同写法，实际上并不冲突：两者的 KL 梯度项在这里样本级完全相同。

也就是说，在不考虑 baseline / advantage 的具体构造细节时：

  • “把 KL 写进 loss 并用低方差实现（⟦INLMATH479MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH480MATHEND⟧）”
  • 与”把 KL 写进 reward 并选 ⟦INLMATH481MATHEND⟧（stop-grad shaped reward）”

对策略更新施加的 KL 正则”力”可以完全相同。

具体来说，如果只看”最大化 ⟦INLMATH482MATHEND⟧”时 KL 惩罚贡献的那一项梯度（惩罚项在 ⟦INLMATH483MATHEND⟧ 里带负号，因此这项的上升方向自然带 ⟦INLMATH484MATHEND⟧）：

  • 损失项写法（低方差实现）：⟦INLMATH485MATHEND⟧；
  • 奖励塑形写法（⟦INLMATH486MATHEND⟧ in reward）：⟦INLMATH487MATHEND⟧。

这是同一个随机变量，所以不仅期望相同，方差也完全相同。

整体更新语义的差异

虽然 KL 梯度项在样本级等价，两种写法的整体更新语义仍然不同。差异主要体现在以下几个方面：

1. KL 是否进入 Advantage / Baseline

KL 作为损失项（等价于最大化 ⟦INLMATH488MATHEND⟧，但把 KL 项作为一个独立、可控的”显式力”来实现）：

⟦DISPMATH65MATHEND⟧

KL 是一个独立的正则项，与 advantage 完全解耦。KL 梯度的大小只取决于 ⟦INLMATH489MATHEND⟧ 本身，不受 critic 质量或 baseline 选择的影响。

KL 作为奖励塑形项：

⟦DISPMATH66MATHEND⟧

KL 通过 shaped reward 进入 advantage 计算，会被 baseline 处理。这意味着：

  • KL 的影响会被 advantage 的构造方式调制；
  • 如果使用 value function baseline，KL 的影响会被部分吸收。

从实现角度看，这里的差别可以理解为：Loss 方案把”环境回报部分”和”KL 正则部分”分开估计；Reward 方案把 KL 视为回报的一部分，所以它会跟着你对回报做的所有处理（baseline、归一化、截断等）一起走。

2. 信用分配：独立正则力 vs 混入 Shaped Reward

KL 作为损失项：每个 token / state 的 KL 梯度是”局部”的，只影响该位置的策略更新。

KL 作为奖励塑形项：KL 惩罚通过 return / advantage 的时间回传，可能影响到更早的决策。

3. Reward 中心化 KL：对梯度无偏性的影响

在大模型 RL（如 GRPO、PPO for LLM）中，常见的 advantage 算法是 ⟦INLMATH490MATHEND⟧。当 KL 作为奖励塑形项时，是否把 KL 也纳入 mean 会影响梯度的无偏性。

设采样 ⟦INLMATH491MATHEND⟧，记 ⟦INLMATH492MATHEND⟧，并用 ⟦INLMATH493MATHEND⟧ 表示第 ⟦INLMATH494MATHEND⟧ 个样本的 KL 惩罚标量，⟦INLMATH495MATHEND⟧。

不中心化（⟦INLMATH496MATHEND⟧）：KL 梯度项的期望为

⟦DISPMATH67MATHEND⟧

这是对 ⟦INLMATH497MATHEND⟧ 的无偏梯度。

同 batch 均值中心化（⟦INLMATH498MATHEND⟧，含自身）：由于 ⟦INLMATH499MATHEND⟧ 依赖所有样本（包括 ⟦INLMATH500MATHEND⟧ 自身），期望梯度变为

⟦DISPMATH68MATHEND⟧

即 KL 正则梯度被缩小了 ⟦INLMATH501MATHEND⟧，等价于有效 ⟦INLMATH502MATHEND⟧ 变小。这不是严格无偏的。

Leave-one-out 中心化（⟦INLMATH503MATHEND⟧）：若改用 ⟦INLMATH504MATHEND⟧，则 ⟦INLMATH505MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH506MATHEND⟧ 独立，有 ⟦INLMATH507MATHEND⟧，因此

⟦DISPMATH69MATHEND⟧

仍是无偏梯度，同时享受中心化带来的方差缩减。

同 batch 均值中心化引入的偏差为 ⟦INLMATH508MATHEND⟧，在 GRPO 等大 batch 场景下影响很小；若追求严格无偏，可改用 leave-one-out 均值，还能享受方差缩减。

何时选择哪种方式？

维度 KL 作为损失项 KL 作为奖励塑形项
KL 梯度形态 ⟦INLMATH509MATHEND⟧（低方差选择） ⟦INLMATH510MATHEND⟧
与 Advantage 完全解耦 通过 shaped reward 耦合
KL 中心化 无（绝对惩罚） 有（⟦INLMATH511MATHEND⟧）
信用分配 局部、per-token 可能有时间回传（取决于实现）
适用场景 希望 KL 作为显式正则项单独控制 希望 KL 随 shaped reward 一起进入 advantage / return

实践建议：

  1. 希望 KL 作为显式正则项单独控制——也就是尽量少受 advantage 构造、critic / baseline 质量影响，那就选 KL 作为损失项，使用 ⟦INLMATH512MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH513MATHEND⟧。注意，on-policy 时如果不想显式构造 ⟦INLMATH514MATHEND⟧，直接用 ⟦INLMATH515MATHEND⟧ 更简单也不易出错。

  2. 希望 KL 随 shaped reward 一起进入 advantage / return——也就是接受它与 baseline、信用分配路径耦合，那就选 KL 作为奖励塑形项，使用 ⟦INLMATH516MATHEND⟧。

基于上面”数值无偏 vs 梯度正确”以及”Loss 与 Reward 实现差异”的结论，下面给出可直接照抄到代码里的选型速查与常见踩坑点。

9. 实践指南与常见陷阱

9.1 三种估计器定义速查

⟦DISPMATH70MATHEND⟧

9.2 数值估计性质

估计器 对反向 KL ⟦INLMATH517MATHEND⟧ 数值无偏？ 方差
⟦INLMATH518MATHEND⟧ ✓ 高（估计值可负）
⟦INLMATH519MATHEND⟧ ✗（小 KL 邻域通常偏差较小） 低
⟦INLMATH520MATHEND⟧ ✓ 低

9.3 常见陷阱

  1. On-policy 下用 ⟦INLMATH521MATHEND⟧ 作为损失项：梯度期望为零，完全无效。
  2. On-policy 下用 ⟦INLMATH522MATHEND⟧ 作为损失项来优化反向 KL：若目标是反向 KL，其梯度实际对应的是正向 KL ⟦INLMATH523MATHEND⟧。
  3. Off-policy 下用 ⟦INLMATH524MATHEND⟧（重要性权重不 detach）：梯度对应一个只在局部二阶行为上与 KL 一致的 surrogate，而非反向 KL。
  4. 在奖励塑形项里使用 ⟦INLMATH525MATHEND⟧：虽然数值无偏，但诱导的策略梯度有偏，实践中可能显著不稳定甚至崩溃。
  5. On-policy 时把 ⟦INLMATH526MATHEND⟧ 简单设为常数 1：必须显式构造 ⟦INLMATH527MATHEND⟧（或等价地 ⟦INLMATH528MATHEND⟧），否则会丢掉 score-function 梯度路径，使 ⟦INLMATH529MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH530MATHEND⟧ 退化为朴素形式，不再对应前文的无偏结论。
  6. 混淆”数值无偏”与”梯度正确”：⟦INLMATH531MATHEND⟧ 对反向 KL 数值无偏，但作为奖励塑形项时诱导的策略梯度有偏；选择估计器时必须同时考虑这两个维度。

10. 总结

如果只记住四句话，可以记这四句：

  1. 数值无偏不等于梯度正确。 选 KL 估计器时，不仅要看它把 KL 数值估得准不准，还要看它在你的具体写法里到底在优化谁。
  2. 若把 KL 写成可微损失项：on-policy 的朴素实现直接用 ⟦INLMATH532MATHEND⟧ 最省心；若显式构造 ⟦INLMATH533MATHEND⟧，或本来就是 off-policy，则推荐 ⟦INLMATH534MATHEND⟧ 或 ⟦INLMATH535MATHEND⟧。
  3. 若把 KL 写成 stop-grad reward shaping：在本文讨论的策略梯度主项里，只有 ⟦INLMATH536MATHEND⟧ 能保持与反向 KL 正则一致的无偏梯度。
  4. 低方差 KL loss 与 ⟦INLMATH537MATHEND⟧ in reward 在 KL 那一项上可以样本级等价，但整体算法语义并不相同。 前者把 KL 当作独立正则项；后者会把 KL 带进 advantage、baseline 和信用分配路径里。

11. 参考文献

  1. Dibya Ghosh. “KL Divergence for Machine Learning”. https://dibyaghosh.com/blog/probability/kldivergence
  2. John Schulman. “Approximating KL Divergence”. https://joschu.net/blog/kl-approx.html
  3. Verl Documentation. “Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)”. https://verl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/algo/ppo.html
  4. 初七123334. “RLHF/RLVR 训练中的 KL 近似方法浅析（k1 / k2 / k3）”. https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/1966872846212010437
  5. Kezhao Liu, Jason Klein Liu, Mingtao Chen, Yiming Liu. “Rethinking KL Regularization in RLHF: From Value Estimation to Gradient Optimization”. arXiv:2510.01555. https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.01555
  6. Yifan Zhang, Yiping Ji, Gavin Brown, et al. “On the Design of KL-Regularized Policy Gradient Algorithms for LLM Reasoning”. arXiv:2505.17508. https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.17508
  7. Vedant Shah, Johan Obando-Ceron, Vineet Jain, Brian Bartoldson, Bhavya Kailkhura, Sarthak Mittal, Glen Berseth, Pablo Samuel Castro. “A Comedy of Estimators: On KL Regularization in RL Training of LLMs”. arXiv:2512.21852. https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.21852
@misc{WangZhang2025KLEstimators,
  author       = {Wang, Xihuai and Zhang, Shao},
  title        = {Choosing KL Estimators in RL: From Value Unbiasedness to Gradient Correctness},
  year         = {2025},
  month        = dec,
  day          = {01},
  url          = {https://xihuai18.github.io/reinforcement-learning/2025/12/01/kl-estimators-zh.html}
}
entry.summary: 在强化学习中，KL 估计器不能只看数值估得准不准，还要看它在 loss 或 reward 写法下究竟优化了谁。本文比较 k1、k2、k3 在 on-policy 与 off-policy 场景中的差异，并给出可直接落地的选型建议。

entry.title: From Two Policies to Three: Extending TRPO under Behavior–Reference Policy Mismatch in LLM RL

link.title: From Two Policies to Three: Extending TRPO under Behavior–Reference Policy Mismatch in LLM RL

entry.content:
1. Training–Inference Mismatch and Asynchronous Frameworks

Recent LLM RL work has repeatedly run into the same issue: the behavior policy that actually generates data may not match the reference policy used in training.

This note first reviews the pieces of prior work that matter most for the story, then organizes them around that single mismatch.

I am not trying to prove a stronger TRPO theorem here. What I care about is separating three policies that are often collapsed together in LLM RL, and using that split to say more precisely what training-inference mismatch is actually breaking.

More concretely, this note does only three things:

  • rewrites the usual TRPO surrogate gap with the behavior policy as the baseline;
  • upper-bounds that gap using behavior, reference, and target policies simultaneously;
  • uses that decomposition to reread training-inference mismatch, sample-level correction, and routing replay in LLM RL.

Throughout the note I’ll use:

  • Behavior policy ⟦INLMATH48MATHEND⟧: the policy that actually generates rollouts, i.e., “under which distribution your data are sampled.” In modern LLM RL systems this typically corresponds to the implementation inside the inference engine (vLLM, SGLang, etc.), and under asynchronous frameworks it is often a mixture distribution over multiple worker policies.

  • Reference policy ⟦INLMATH49MATHEND⟧: the policy used in the training objective for importance sampling, clipping, or KL constraints — typically the “old policy” in PPO / GRPO.

  • Target policy ⟦INLMATH50MATHEND⟧: the policy we optimize in the training objective, i.e., “what we want the model to become” — typically the “new policy” in PPO / GRPO.

In the classical idealized setup, we usually implicitly assume ⟦INLMATH51MATHEND⟧. In real systems, however, asynchronous updates, different inference / training backends, MoE routing fluctuations, and even hardware-level numerical differences cause these two policies to deviate to varying degrees.

2. Related Work

Below I organize the relevant works along three threads:

  • Algorithmic layer: how to write the trust-region objective and handle “behavior vs. reference” mismatch at the sample level;
  • Systems layer: how to keep the behavior policy close to the reference policy from outside the algorithm;
  • Model layer: MoE-specific issues from expert routing.

A single paper can touch multiple layers; I place each one under its main contribution.

2.1 Algorithmic layer: objective and sample-level mechanisms

  • Decoupled PPO was among the first to point out that in trust-region policy optimization methods (TRPO and PPO), the “old policy” actually plays two distinct roles:

    1. It is used for importance sampling to perform off-policy correction. In this sense, the “old policy” is meant to represent the behavior policy that generated the training data.

    2. It is also used to limit the update step size of the new policy. In this sense, the “old policy” acts as a baseline to measure how much the new and old policies differ, i.e., a proximal policy (what I call the reference policy here).

    The paper points out that these two roles do not have to be played by the same policy, and proposes the Decoupled PPO objective, which explicitly decouples “who generates the data” from “who defines the trust region” at the level of the optimization objective.

  • GSPO starts from stability issues of GRPO on long sequences and MoE models. It shows that token-level PPO / GRPO can become highly unstable when MoE expert routing is extremely volatile (especially when routing differs significantly between old and new policies), leading to large variance and training collapse. GSPO proposes a sequence-level PPO-style objective and ratio constraint, using the ratio over entire sequences to control updates. This substantially mitigates training collapse in MoE scenarios caused by routing instability and token-level noise.

  • Your Efficient RL Framework Secretly Brings You Off-Policy RL Training observes that in existing LLM RL frameworks (such as VeRL), the inference stack and the training stack often differ across multiple functional modules (e.g., vLLM vs. FSDP / Megatron kernels and operators). This makes the behavior policy ⟦INLMATH52MATHEND⟧ differ from the reference policy ⟦INLMATH53MATHEND⟧, so what is assumed to be on-policy training actually becomes off-policy training with nontrivial bias. The article summarizes two existing ways to handle this: PPO-IS and vanilla-IS, and further proposes token-level truncated importance sampling (TIS) to downweight samples with severe training–inference mismatch. The author also wrote two more foundational notes analyzing training–inference mismatch from basic principles: Part I and Part II.

  • Small Leak Can Sink a Great Ship—Boost RL Training on MoE with 𝑰𝒄𝒆𝑷𝒐𝒑! observes that the above mismatch issues are further amplified in MoE models: routing itself is highly sensitive to small perturbations, and stacked with inference / training implementation differences and asynchronous sampling, it is easy to magnify bias and instability. The paper proposes IcePop: at the token level, it computes importance sampling ratios and applies two-sided masking to discard tokens whose ratios are either too large or too small. This removes “very noisy” data from the gradient, stabilizing RL training on MoE models.

  • When Speed Kills Stability: Demystifying RL Collapse from the Training-Inference Mismatch gives a systematic analysis of the causes of training–inference mismatch, including large amounts of out-of-distribution and low-probability content introduced by agent workflows, hardware and kernel-level numerical uncertainty, and how token-level importance sampling can introduce severe bias on long sequences. It further proposes sequence-level masked importance sampling (sequence-level MIS): compute an IS ratio at the sequence level and discard only those sequences whose overall ratio is too large, thereby controlling bias while strongly suppressing training collapse caused by extreme samples. The paper provides reasonably complete theoretical derivations and extensive experimental evidence.

  • verl Rollout Importance Sampling introduces a Token Veto mechanism in its rollout correction module: it computes token-level importance ratios ⟦INLMATH54MATHEND⟧, and if any token in a trajectory satisfies ⟦INLMATH55MATHEND⟧, the entire sequence is discarded from training. This “token-level detection, sequence-level veto” design embodies a conservative “one-vote veto” strategy.

  • INTELLECT-3 Technical Report adopts a similar rejection sampling strategy in its asynchronous distributed RL training framework. INTELLECT-3 computes token-level importance ratios for each rollout; if any token’s ratio falls below a threshold (⟦INLMATH56MATHEND⟧ in the paper), the entire trajectory is masked.

2.2 Systems layer: asynchrony and training–inference alignment

  • AReaL focuses on the mismatch between behavior and reference policies under asynchronous training frameworks: rollouts are often generated by stale parameter versions or different workers. The paper adopts a Decoupled-PPO-style objective in the asynchronous setting, explicitly separating the behavior distribution from the reference policy, while still maintaining PPO-like optimization properties in this asynchronous regime.

  • Defeating Nondeterminism in LLM Inference points out that the lack of batch-size invariance is a core source of randomness in LLM inference: the same input can yield noticeably different probability distributions under different batch compositions and kernel paths. This means that even when you “nominally” have a single set of parameters, the behavior policy ⟦INLMATH57MATHEND⟧ realized in practice can fluctuate with system load and scheduling, further exacerbating training–inference mismatch.

  • RL 老训崩？训推差异是基石 approaches the problem more from a practical perspective, sharing experience on how to engineer for near training–inference consistency: choosing consistent operators and precision settings, monitoring and constraining the log-prob gap between training and inference, etc. The focus is on framework-level engineering practices that can mitigate training–inference difference at the root.

2.3 Model layer: MoE routing

  • Stabilizing MoE Reinforcement Learning by Aligning Training and Inference Routers focuses on the MoE-specific problem of routing inconsistency. The paper finds that even for identical inputs, inference and training can route tokens to different experts due to small differences in operator implementations or parallelism. This “physical-path” mismatch makes the gap between the behavior policy ⟦INLMATH58MATHEND⟧ and the reference policy ⟦INLMATH59MATHEND⟧ much larger than expected and can easily cause training collapse. To address this, the paper proposes Rollout Routing Replay (R3): during rollout it records, for each token, the actual expert indices selected by the inference router, and during training it replays these routing decisions instead of recomputing them. In effect, R3 forces the training and inference stacks to share the same routing paths in the MoE topology, aligning the two sides at the level of the computation graph.

3. Three-Policy TRPO: A Minimal Unifying Frame

These works span different layers, but organized along a single axis — behavior policy vs. reference policy — most of them fit inside one minimal frame: three-policy TRPO.

The derivation is short. First write the surrogate gap with the behavior policy as baseline; then add one triangle inequality and the two mismatch sources appear. What surprised me is how useful this tiny split is once you start looking at modern LLM RL systems through it:

  • On the one hand, it helps us understand what exactly “training–inference mismatch” and “asynchronous training frameworks” are harming within the TRPO view.
  • On the other hand, it offers a unifying way to interpret TIS, IcePop, sequence-level MIS, etc. In the view of this note, these methods mostly mitigate the estimation bias/variance consequences of behavior-reference mismatch, rather than directly changing the worst-case value of ⟦INLMATH60MATHEND⟧.

3.1 Three Policies

We stick to the notation from above and consider a discounted MDP with discount factor ⟦INLMATH61MATHEND⟧:

For LLM RL, many workloads are closer to finite-horizon sequence decision problems than to infinite-horizon discounted MDPs. I still use the discounted-MDP notation here because it connects most directly to the classical TRPO derivation; the same decomposition carries over to finite-horizon versions as well.

  • States ⟦INLMATH62MATHEND⟧, actions ⟦INLMATH63MATHEND⟧.
  • Policy ⟦INLMATH64MATHEND⟧.
  • Discounted state distribution: ⟦DISPMATH10MATHEND⟧
  • Return (episodic view): ⟦DISPMATH11MATHEND⟧
  • Value / Q / advantage functions: ⟦DISPMATH12MATHEND⟧

I keep the notation from the introduction: behavior policy ⟦INLMATH65MATHEND⟧, reference policy ⟦INLMATH66MATHEND⟧, and target policy ⟦INLMATH67MATHEND⟧.

In the ideal setup we assume ⟦INLMATH68MATHEND⟧; in real systems they are often unequal. This is the mathematical shadow of “training–inference mismatch.”

3.2 Two-Policy TRPO: A Behavior-Baselined Surrogate-Gap Bound

If you’re already familiar with TRPO, feel free to skip ahead to the “Three-Policy TRPO” subsection.

I keep the core TRPO logic, but I will not try to reproduce the exact theorem statement from Schulman et al. (2015). For the three-policy story below, a looser behavior-baselined surrogate-gap bound is the more useful object.

All the theoretical guarantees in TRPO are stated with respect to the advantage function of some baseline policy. Here I take ⟦INLMATH69MATHEND⟧ as the baseline for two reasons: the data are sampled under ⟦INLMATH70MATHEND⟧, and in practice the critic / GAE / group-normalized reward proxy we can estimate most naturally from those data is also anchored to that behavior distribution. I ignore the approximation error in estimating ⟦INLMATH71MATHEND⟧ itself and focus only on the policy-mismatch part.

A classical result is the Performance Difference Lemma:

For any two policies ⟦INLMATH72MATHEND⟧ and ⟦INLMATH73MATHEND⟧, we have

⟦DISPMATH1MATHEND⟧

The challenge in TRPO is that we cannot compute

⟦DISPMATH13MATHEND⟧

exactly, because ⟦INLMATH74MATHEND⟧ is the state distribution of the new policy, under which we do not have samples.

So TRPO introduces a surrogate objective by replacing the state distribution with that of the behavior policy:

⟦DISPMATH14MATHEND⟧

In actual PPO / GRPO-style implementations, this expectation is usually rewritten using importance sampling. That is also where a second policy often enters the picture: the denominator in the ratio is frequently the reference policy ⟦INLMATH75MATHEND⟧ rather than the true behavior policy ⟦INLMATH76MATHEND⟧. The gap between those two is exactly what will become ⟦INLMATH77MATHEND⟧ later.

Starting from the Performance Difference Lemma, the difference between the true objective and the surrogate is:

⟦DISPMATH15MATHEND⟧

If we define

⟦DISPMATH16MATHEND⟧

we immediately get the following upper bound:

Lemma 1

⟦DISPMATH2MATHEND⟧

This reveals the first key quantity:

State distribution shift ⟦INLMATH78MATHEND⟧, i.e., “how differently the new policy sees the world, compared to the behavior policy.”

We usually do not directly impose constraints on ⟦INLMATH79MATHEND⟧. Instead, we constrain the per-timestep action distribution difference — via trust regions, KL penalties, clipping, etc.

Define the total variation (TV) distance:

⟦DISPMATH17MATHEND⟧

Assume there is a constant ⟦INLMATH80MATHEND⟧ such that

For all ⟦INLMATH81MATHEND⟧, the TV distance between the behavior and target policies is bounded:

⟦DISPMATH3MATHEND⟧

Intuitively: in any state, the action distribution of the “new policy” cannot deviate too much from that of the policy that generated the data.

A standard result (provable via coupling) is:

Lemma 2 Under the assumption above,

⟦DISPMATH4MATHEND⟧

Combining Lemma 1 and Lemma 2, we obtain

⟦DISPMATH18MATHEND⟧

This gives a compact two-policy surrogate-gap bound with the behavior policy as baseline:

Theorem 1 (Behavior-Baselined Two-Policy Bound)

⟦DISPMATH5MATHEND⟧

This suggests:

  • What really matters for the tightness of ⟦INLMATH82MATHEND⟧ as a surrogate for ⟦INLMATH83MATHEND⟧ is how far the behavior policy ⟦INLMATH84MATHEND⟧ and the target policy ⟦INLMATH85MATHEND⟧ drift apart: ⟦DISPMATH19MATHEND⟧

If you can directly control this ⟦INLMATH86MATHEND⟧, you can essentially port TRPO’s monotonic-improvement logic to the behavior-policy view. This is a deliberately conservative worst-case-TV presentation: the goal here is to make the structure transparent, not to optimize constants. If you switch to an average-TV version, you get a similar conclusion closer to sample averages, with modified constants and expectations.

3.3 Three-Policy TRPO

In practice, especially in large-scale LLM RL, we often cannot directly control ⟦INLMATH87MATHEND⟧ itself.

In most PPO / GRPO / GSPO / RLHF-style frameworks, the actual situation is:

  • Rollout data are generated by some behavior policy ⟦INLMATH88MATHEND⟧ (some particular parameter version plus system details inside the inference engine).
  • During updates, we would like to leverage a reference policy ⟦INLMATH89MATHEND⟧ to limit the update of the target policy ⟦INLMATH90MATHEND⟧.

Practically, the quantities we can directly control or indirectly influence are:

  1. Reference vs. target: via KL penalties, clipping, etc., we constrain

    ⟦DISPMATH20MATHEND⟧

  2. Behavior vs. reference: we would like to keep ⟦DISPMATH21MATHEND⟧ small as well — this is where training–inference mismatch and asynchronous execution come in.

This motivates two deviation sources, corresponding to two TV-distance terms:

  • Deviation source A: reference vs. target

    ⟦DISPMATH22MATHEND⟧

  • Deviation source B: behavior vs. reference ⟦DISPMATH23MATHEND⟧

Intuitively:

  • ⟦INLMATH91MATHEND⟧: how far the new policy is from the reference policy chosen in the loss — this is the trust-region part.
  • ⟦INLMATH92MATHEND⟧: how far the reference policy used in training is from the actual behavior policy that generated the data — this is the footprint of training–inference mismatch and asynchrony.

Now we can plug these two quantities back into the TRPO lower bound.

For any state ⟦INLMATH93MATHEND⟧, by the triangle inequality we have

⟦DISPMATH24MATHEND⟧

Taking the supremum over ⟦INLMATH94MATHEND⟧ gives

⟦DISPMATH25MATHEND⟧

Plugging this inequality into the two-policy TRPO bound (Theorem 1), and denoting

⟦DISPMATH26MATHEND⟧

we obtain

⟦DISPMATH27MATHEND⟧

This yields a very direct three-policy TRPO lower bound:

Theorem 2 (Three-Policy TRPO) Let

⟦DISPMATH6MATHEND⟧

and

⟦DISPMATH7MATHEND⟧

Then for any target policy ⟦INLMATH95MATHEND⟧,

⟦DISPMATH8MATHEND⟧

where

⟦DISPMATH9MATHEND⟧

The point of Theorem 2 is simple: the gap between ⟦INLMATH96MATHEND⟧ and ⟦INLMATH97MATHEND⟧ is not exactly decomposed into two terms, but it is controlled by a conservative upper bound involving both ⟦INLMATH98MATHEND⟧ and ⟦INLMATH99MATHEND⟧. To get improvement you still need ⟦INLMATH100MATHEND⟧ itself to be large enough. Numerically this bound is often loose in LLM settings, so I read it mainly as a structural tool rather than a performance certificate.

3.4 How to Control These Two Deviation Sources?

We can now revisit various practical methods through the lens of Theorem 2:

  • Most PPO / GRPO-style work focuses on the reference-vs.-target side, i.e. ⟦INLMATH101MATHEND⟧.
  • GSPO belongs to the same side, but it does something stronger than “control ⟦INLMATH102MATHEND⟧ more carefully”: it changes the basic unit of ratio, clipping, and optimization from token level to sequence level.
  • TIS / IcePop / MIS / WTRS mostly operate on sample weights, sample subsets, or rejection rules to mitigate the estimation consequences of behavior-vs.-reference mismatch, rather than directly changing the worst-case definition of ⟦INLMATH103MATHEND⟧.

If I had to say which side worries me more in practice, it is almost always the latter: many systems drift out of the “approximately on-policy” regime because behavior and reference policies have already diverged before the trust-region machinery itself visibly fails. In the remainder of this note I focus on the behavior-vs.-reference side.

The goal on this side is simple: make the effective training data come from a behavior policy close enough to the reference policy, or at least prevent badly mismatched samples from dominating the gradient.

In practice, this usually involves both system-level mechanisms and algorithmic mechanisms (importance sampling).

  1. System level: keep behavior and reference policies close

    • Asynchronous frameworks: Tag each sample with a policy version, and only use data generated by parameter versions that are close enough to ⟦INLMATH104MATHEND⟧.

    • Training–inference alignment: Use consistent precision, operators, and similar kernel behavior between the training and inference stacks.

    These mechanisms act “outside” the algorithm to make ⟦INLMATH105MATHEND⟧ closer to ⟦INLMATH106MATHEND⟧, thereby reducing ⟦INLMATH107MATHEND⟧ more directly.

  2. Algorithmic level: sample-wise correction

    At the algorithmic level, we no longer attempt to “fix” the entire behavior policy. Instead, we use importance sampling ratios to filter or reweight samples at the sample level, so that the subset of data that actually participates in training is closer to the reference policy, or at least so that badly mismatched samples carry less weight.

    Concretely, this gives rise to methods like TIS, IcePop, MIS, and WTRS. They are best understood as different ways to manage the consequences of this mismatch at training time.

4. Importance Sampling and Masking: Four Sample-Level Responses to Behavior-Reference Mismatch

In this section I reuse the notation above to write down four representative mechanisms, focusing only on the design choices related to “behavior vs. reference policy.” The unified notation below is meant to highlight how this mismatch is handled in the training loss, not to reproduce every implementation detail from each paper (advantage estimation, baselines, extra regularizers, and so on). The losses ⟦INLMATH108MATHEND⟧ are therefore comparison devices, not literal unbiased estimators of the theoretical surrogate ⟦INLMATH109MATHEND⟧. Let the token-level PPO / GRPO-style update term be

⟦DISPMATH28MATHEND⟧

where

⟦DISPMATH29MATHEND⟧

Here:

  • ⟦INLMATH110MATHEND⟧ is the target-vs.-reference ratio.
  • ⟦INLMATH111MATHEND⟧ denotes the practical advantage term corresponding to the theoretical ⟦INLMATH112MATHEND⟧. In real implementations it typically comes from a critic / GAE / group-normalized reward proxy estimated from ⟦INLMATH113MATHEND⟧-sampled data, so it should not be read as a literal unbiased replacement for ⟦INLMATH114MATHEND⟧.

To connect token-level ⟦INLMATH115MATHEND⟧ with sequence-level ⟦INLMATH116MATHEND⟧ notation, consider the RLHF setting (reinforcement learning from human feedback) for LLMs:

  • Prompts are denoted by ⟦INLMATH117MATHEND⟧, and responses by ⟦INLMATH118MATHEND⟧.
  • Token-level states and actions are defined as ⟦INLMATH119MATHEND⟧, ⟦INLMATH120MATHEND⟧.
  • The behavior and reference policies on sequences can then be written as ⟦DISPMATH30MATHEND⟧

To quantify the deviation between reference and behavior policies, we can define the token-level importance ratio:

⟦DISPMATH31MATHEND⟧

and its sequence-level counterpart:

⟦DISPMATH32MATHEND⟧

The difference between TIS, IcePop, and MIS lies in how they use ⟦INLMATH121MATHEND⟧ to manage behavior-reference mismatch during training.

4.1 TIS: Token-Level Truncated Importance Sampling

TIS directly truncates the token-level ratio ⟦INLMATH122MATHEND⟧; define

⟦DISPMATH33MATHEND⟧

The update objective becomes

⟦DISPMATH34MATHEND⟧

  • The blue ⟦INLMATH123MATHEND⟧ is the truncated IS weight: extremely large ratios are capped at a constant ⟦INLMATH124MATHEND⟧.
  • From the three-policy TRPO perspective, this is a soft way to downweight tokens where behavior and reference policies differ significantly, thereby reducing their influence on the effective training distribution.
  • Note that ⟦INLMATH125MATHEND⟧ truncates the behavior-to-reference ratio, while the clipping inside ⟦INLMATH126MATHEND⟧ controls the reference-to-target ratio. These are two separate operations.

4.2 IcePop: Token-Level Two-Sided Masking in MoE

IcePop also uses ⟦INLMATH127MATHEND⟧ as a discrepancy measure, but opts for two-sided masking:

⟦DISPMATH35MATHEND⟧

The update objective becomes

⟦DISPMATH36MATHEND⟧

  • The blue ⟦INLMATH128MATHEND⟧ decides whether a token participates in the update: tokens with ratios that are too large or too small are dropped entirely.
  • This is a hard sample selection scheme: only tokens where behavior and reference policies are reasonably aligned (ratios within ⟦INLMATH129MATHEND⟧) are kept.

4.3 Sequence-Level MIS: Masked Importance Sampling Over Entire Sequences

The core operation in sequence-level MIS is to retain only sequences whose sequence-level IS ratio is below a threshold ⟦INLMATH130MATHEND⟧, zeroing out the loss for all other sequences:

⟦DISPMATH37MATHEND⟧

In a unified loss form, this can be written as

⟦DISPMATH38MATHEND⟧

From the three-policy TRPO viewpoint, sequence-level MIS no longer truncates at the token level. Instead, it performs trajectory-level filtering: it drops trajectories where behavior and reference policies diverge too much, and only optimizes on the subset with ⟦INLMATH131MATHEND⟧. My own bias is that once sequences get long, this sequence-level treatment is usually more honest than token-level patching, because token-level weights get dominated by extremes too easily.

Note: In this unified form, ⟦INLMATH132MATHEND⟧ handles the sequence-level correction from behavior to reference, while ⟦INLMATH133MATHEND⟧ still handles the token-level update from reference to target. In practice, implementations often add extra truncation or stabilization on top of this basic structure.

4.4 A Veto-Style Extreme: What I Call WTRS

The verl Token Veto mechanism and INTELLECT-3 both use a veto-style rejection idea. For the sake of analysis, I will refer to this shared pattern as Worst Token Reject Sampling (WTRS). This is my umbrella term, not a standard name from either source paper, and the concrete implementations are not identical.

Under that abstraction:

  • verl Token Veto: In its rollout correction module, if any token in a trajectory has ⟦INLMATH134MATHEND⟧, the entire sequence is discarded via response_mask. The threshold ⟦INLMATH135MATHEND⟧ is user-configurable.

  • INTELLECT-3 Token Masking: In its asynchronous distributed RL framework, if any token’s ratio is below ⟦INLMATH136MATHEND⟧, the entire trajectory is masked.

The core operation is identical: if any token in a trajectory has an IS ratio below a threshold ⟦INLMATH137MATHEND⟧, the entire sequence is rejected from training. This can be written as:

⟦DISPMATH39MATHEND⟧

In a unified loss form:

⟦DISPMATH40MATHEND⟧

From the three-policy TRPO perspective, WTRS adopts a hybrid “token-level detection, sequence-level veto” strategy: it detects extreme mismatch signals at the token level, and once detected, rejects at the sequence level. This is aggressively conservative. The price is poor sample efficiency; the benefit is that under severe system noise it can be more stable than trying to rescue bad trajectories token by token.

5. MoE Routing Replay: What Does It Actually Do in Three-Policy TRPO?

In MoE (Mixture-of-Experts) models, training–inference mismatch often first appears as routing inconsistency: even with identical parameters, the inference and training stacks may route tokens to different experts because of small differences in operators, parallelism, or numerics. A natural engineering response is routing replay: during rollout (inference), record the actual expert paths, and during training, force the model to reuse these routing decisions.

Modeling assumption: in this section I treat routing choice ⟦INLMATH138MATHEND⟧ as part of the action space, i.e. “choose an expert” and “generate a token” are modeled as a joint decision. Everything below about routing replay as an objective rewrite is conditional on that modeling choice.

These methods are often described as “implementing behavior-reference correction” or even “shrinking ⟦INLMATH139MATHEND⟧.” From the three-policy TRPO perspective, a more precise statement is:

Routing replay does not directly shrink the original policy-distance terms; instead, it rewrites the surrogate objective into one that is conditioned on / replaces the routing. It makes routing mismatch invisible in the loss, but it does not actually shrink the true policy distances ⟦INLMATH140MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH141MATHEND⟧.

Below I’ll sketch a minimal abstraction that is sufficient to make this concrete.

5.1 Surrogate Objective in MoE: Separating Routing and Token Generation

Abstract an MoE model as a two-stage stochastic decision: “first choose an expert ⟦INLMATH142MATHEND⟧, then generate token ⟦INLMATH143MATHEND⟧ conditioned on that expert.” The target policy can be factorized as

⟦DISPMATH41MATHEND⟧

where:

  • ⟦INLMATH144MATHEND⟧ is the router distribution.
  • ⟦INLMATH145MATHEND⟧ is the token distribution conditioned on expert ⟦INLMATH146MATHEND⟧.

In the three-policy TRPO setting, the surrogate objective we actually want to optimize can be written as

⟦DISPMATH42MATHEND⟧

where I use

⟦DISPMATH43MATHEND⟧

to denote the expert-level aggregation of advantages.

Here ⟦INLMATH147MATHEND⟧ denotes the advantage when “choosing expert ⟦INLMATH148MATHEND⟧ and then generating token ⟦INLMATH149MATHEND⟧” is treated as a joint decision. This section therefore works with an extended model in which routing is made explicit inside the decision process.

The key point is that in the original ⟦INLMATH150MATHEND⟧, the routing distribution is precisely the current router ⟦INLMATH151MATHEND⟧ that we are updating. RL on MoE is therefore updating not only the token-generation distribution but also the router itself.

5.2 Replaying Behavior-Policy Routing (Behavior-Router Replay / R3-Style)

R3-style methods record, during rollout, the set of experts ⟦INLMATH152MATHEND⟧ actually selected by the behavior policy on the inference side, and during training force the current policy to route only within this set. This can be written as a “conditional projection” of the routing distribution:

⟦DISPMATH44MATHEND⟧

The surrogate objective that is actually optimized during training becomes

⟦DISPMATH45MATHEND⟧

Compared to the original ⟦INLMATH153MATHEND⟧, R3 does not push ⟦INLMATH154MATHEND⟧ closer to ⟦INLMATH155MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH156MATHEND⟧. Instead, it:

  • replaces the expectation over ⟦INLMATH157MATHEND⟧ by a conditional expectation over ⟦INLMATH158MATHEND⟧, and
  • equivalently, shrinks the feasible routing support to ⟦INLMATH159MATHEND⟧.

So R3 is optimizing a “behavior-routing-conditioned surrogate objective,” rather than the original ⟦INLMATH160MATHEND⟧. The benefit is substantially reduced variance and improved stability; the cost is that the router’s exploration and update freedom is constrained at every state.

5.3 Replaying Reference-Policy Routing (Reference-Router Replay)

Another class of routing-replay schemes instead reuses the reference policy’s router ⟦INLMATH161MATHEND⟧. This is equivalent to training a hybrid policy

⟦DISPMATH46MATHEND⟧

with surrogate objective

⟦DISPMATH47MATHEND⟧

This has the effect that:

  • In the surrogate objective, the router is frozen to the old router ⟦INLMATH162MATHEND⟧, so the “reference vs. target” discrepancy in routing is simply removed from the loss.
  • Training becomes insensitive to how far the new router ⟦INLMATH163MATHEND⟧ drifts from ⟦INLMATH164MATHEND⟧, thereby sidestepping the instabilities caused by routing mismatch.

Again, this is fundamentally a change of objective:

  • The deviation ⟦INLMATH165MATHEND⟧ in the true policy space is not reduced; it is merely rendered invisible by redefining the surrogate in terms of the old router.
  • Learning of the router is effectively frozen or heavily suppressed.

5.4 Routing Replay as a Change of Surrogate Objective

Putting these replay variants side by side, they share several properties:

  1. They optimize not the original ⟦INLMATH166MATHEND⟧, but a surrogate where routing has been conditioned or replaced.
  2. They do not directly shrink the three-policy TRPO bound’s ⟦INLMATH167MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH168MATHEND⟧. Routing mismatch is removed from the loss, but it still exists in the true policy distances.
  3. In practice they trade bias for variance: replay typically lowers variance and improves stability, but may also limit the router’s ability to learn routing patterns that are optimal for the RL objective.

So, in the three-policy TRPO view, a more accurate characterization is:

Routing replay is best thought of as a rewrite of the surrogate objective, not as a direct implementation of a constraint on ⟦INLMATH169MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH170MATHEND⟧.

Put more bluntly: routing replay is often an effective workaround, not a theoretical fix.

6. Discussion

The core claim of this note is:

Many issues around training-inference mismatch and asynchronous training in large-scale LLM RL are easier to understand once you stop collapsing behavior policy and reference policy into the same object; in practice, the missing term is often ⟦INLMATH171MATHEND⟧.

From two policies to three, the actual move is small:

  • We rewrote the TRPO lower bound from an “old vs. new policy” narrative into a “behavior–reference–target” three-policy relationship.

  • We explicitly separated two TV distances:
    • reference vs. target, ⟦INLMATH172MATHEND⟧, corresponding to the KL / clip / trust-region style controls in PPO / GRPO / GSPO;
    • behavior vs. reference, ⟦INLMATH173MATHEND⟧, capturing real-world factors like asynchronous frameworks, training-inference mismatch, MoE routing volatility, kernel-level nondeterminism, etc.
  • This leads to a simple conclusion: The gap between the surrogate ⟦INLMATH174MATHEND⟧ and the true performance ⟦INLMATH175MATHEND⟧ is bounded by ⟦INLMATH176MATHEND⟧.

Under this lens:

  • Decoupled PPO / AReaL can be viewed as formally acknowledging the existence of three policies and explicitly decoupling the behavior distribution from the reference policy in the objective.

  • PPO / GRPO primarily control the reference-vs.-target side; GSPO also lives on that side, but it does so by changing the optimization unit from token level to sequence level.
  • TIS, IcePop, MIS, and WTRS can be seen as different ways of managing the training-time consequences of behavior-reference mismatch:
    • TIS: token-level truncation of IS weights to soften the influence of extreme samples.
    • IcePop: token-level two-sided masking in MoE to hard-drop tokens with severe mismatch.
    • MIS: sequence-level masking to ignore entire trajectories whose behavior–reference mismatch is too large.
    • WTRS: token-level detection of extremely small ratios, rejecting the entire trajectory once such a signal is found.

  • Routing replay (whether replaying behavior routing in R3-style schemes or replaying reference routing) is better viewed as changing the surrogate objective rather than directly implementing a constraint: both variants replace the original ⟦INLMATH177MATHEND⟧ with a routing-conditioned / routing-frozen surrogate, trading off some objective bias and reduced routing learning freedom for lower variance and greater stability, without actually shrinking ⟦INLMATH178MATHEND⟧ or ⟦INLMATH179MATHEND⟧—they simply make routing mismatch invisible in the loss.

  • Engineering advice such as in RL 老训崩？训推差异是基石 and system-level work like Defeating Nondeterminism in LLM Inference can be interpreted as efforts to reduce ⟦INLMATH180MATHEND⟧ on the systems and numerical side, so that the assumptions underlying the algorithms do not break too badly.

One more practical point matters here: Theorem 2 is written in terms of worst-case TV distances, which are almost impossible to observe directly in LLM-scale systems. What you can usually monitor instead are engineering proxies: average KL on logged states, quantiles of token- or sequence-level importance weights, effective sample size (ESS), rejection / masking rate, and staleness across asynchronous workers. The theorem tells you which mismatches matter; the proxies tell you when they are getting out of hand.

If I had to pick one side to watch first in a real system, it would usually be ⟦INLMATH181MATHEND⟧. That side is easier to ignore, and in practice it often breaks the “approximately PPO / TRPO” regime before the nominal trust-region machinery does.

From this perspective, two practical questions seem especially worth chasing:

  • Under what conditions can we still reasonably interpret “LLM RL training” as some approximate form of TRPO / PPO?
  • For a concrete RL system, should we spend effort first on tighter ⟦INLMATH182MATHEND⟧ control or on pushing ⟦INLMATH183MATHEND⟧ back into a tolerable regime? My bias is the latter: once the behavior distribution has drifted too far, a beautiful trust region is constraining the wrong object.

If this note is useful at all, I hope it is because it makes one neglected point harder to ignore: many things that look like “PPO instability” are already broken one step earlier, at ⟦INLMATH184MATHEND⟧. Writing the three policies separately is often the fastest way to see where the real bottleneck sits.

References

  1. John Schulman, Sergey Levine, Philipp Moritz, Michael I. Jordan, Pieter Abbeel. “Trust Region Policy Optimization” (TRPO). arXiv:1502.05477. https://arxiv.org/abs/1502.05477
  2. Jacob Hilton, Karl Cobbe, John Schulman. “Batch size-invariance for policy optimization” (Decoupled PPO). arXiv:2110.00641. https://arxiv.org/abs/2110.00641
  3. Joshua Achiam, David Held, Aviv Tamar, Pieter Abbeel. “Constrained Policy Optimization” (CPO). arXiv:1705.10528. https://arxiv.org/abs/1705.10528
  4. James Queeney, Ioannis Ch. Paschalidis, Christos G. Cassandras. “Generalized Proximal Policy Optimization with Sample Reuse” (GePPO). arXiv:2111.00072. https://arxiv.org/abs/2111.00072
  5. Wei Fu, Jiaxuan Gao, Xujie Shen, et al. “AReaL: A Large-Scale Asynchronous Reinforcement Learning System for Language Reasoning”. arXiv:2505.24298. https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.24298
  6. Chujie Zheng, Shixuan Liu, Mingze Li, et al. “Group Sequence Policy Optimization” (GSPO). arXiv:2507.18071. https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.18071
  7. Wenhan Ma, Hailin Zhang, Liang Zhao, et al. “Stabilizing MoE Reinforcement Learning by Aligning Training and Inference Routers”. arXiv:2510.11370. https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.11370
@misc{WangZhang2025ThreePolicyTRPO,
  author       = {Wang, Xihuai and Zhang, Shao},
  title        = {From Two Policies to Three: Extending TRPO under Behavior-Reference Policy Mismatch in LLM RL},
  year         = {2025},
  month        = nov,
  day          = {15},
  url          = {https://xihuai18.github.io/reinforcement-learning/2025/11/15/three-policy-en.html},
  urldate      = {2025-11-23}
}
entry.summary: In modern LLM RL pipelines, the policy used as the "old policy" in training can differ from the behavior policy that actually generated the rollouts, breaking the usual on-policy assumption. This note rewrites the TRPO lower bound in a three-policy form — behavior, reference, and target — and argues that the surrogate gap is jointly controlled by two mismatch sources.

entry.title: 从两策略到三策略：LLM RL 中行为策略–参考策略不一致下的 TRPO 扩展

link.title: 从两策略到三策略：LLM RL 中行为策略–参考策略不一致下的 TRPO 扩展

entry.content:
本文的核心结论是：当行为策略 ⟦INLMATH48MATHEND⟧、参考策略 ⟦INLMATH49MATHEND⟧ 与目标策略 ⟦INLMATH50MATHEND⟧ 不再重合时，TRPO / PPO 类 surrogate 的可靠性会同时受到”参考 vs 目标”和”行为 vs 参考”两个偏差来源的影响。

1. 引言：训推不一致与异步框架

最近不少 LLM 强化学习工作反复撞上同一个问题：真正生成数据的行为策略（behavior policy）和训练里使用的参考策略（reference policy）并不一致。

本文先梳理与后文最相关的一组工作，再把这一不一致放进同一个分析框架。

这里不打算证明一个更强的 TRPO 定理；我更关心的是把 LLM-RL 里经常被混在一起的三种策略拆开来写，用它解释训推不一致到底在破坏哪一部分分析。

具体来说，本文只做三件事：

  • 先把经典 TRPO 的 surrogate gap 写成以行为策略为基准的形式；
  • 再把行为策略、参考策略和目标策略用一个保守上界串起来；
  • 最后用这个框架重读 LLM-RL 中的训推不一致、样本修正和 routing replay。

本文使用以下记号：

  • 行为策略 ⟦INLMATH51MATHEND⟧：实际负责生成 rollout 的策略，即”数据是在什么分布下采样的”。在现代 LLM-RL 系统中，它对应推理引擎里的实现（vLLM / SGLang 等）；在异步框架下，它往往还可以近似看作多个 worker 策略诱导分布的混合。
  • 参考策略 ⟦INLMATH52MATHEND⟧：训练目标中用于重要性采样、clipping 或 KL 约束的策略，典型的就是 PPO / GRPO 里的”旧策略”（old policy）。
  • 目标策略 ⟦INLMATH53MATHEND⟧：训练目标中要优化的策略，即”希望模型变成什么样”，典型的就是 PPO / GRPO 里的”新策略”（new policy）。

在最理想化的设定里，我们通常默认 ⟦INLMATH54MATHEND⟧。但在真实系统里，由于异步更新、推理和训练后端不同、MoE 路由波动甚至硬件数值差异，二者往往会偏离，程度视系统而定。

2. 相关工作

下面按三条主线整理与后文三策略视角最相关的工作：

  • 算法层：如何写 trust region 目标、如何在样本层面处理”行为 vs 参考”偏移；
  • 系统层：如何在算法外部让行为策略与参考策略保持接近；
  • 模型层：MoE 路由带来的特殊问题。

同一篇工作可能兼具多个层面，这里按其主要贡献归位。

2.1 算法层：目标函数与样本级机制

  • Decoupled PPO 较早指出，在信赖域策略优化（TRPO 和 PPO）方法中，”旧策略”（old policy）其实同时扮演两个角色：一是用于重要性采样以做异策略修正，此时”旧策略”代表数据所服从的行为策略（behavior policy）；二是用于限制新策略的更新幅度，此时”旧策略”用于衡量新旧策略的变化幅度，称为近端策略（proximal policy，对应本文中的”参考策略”）。文章指出这两个目的下的”旧策略”未必要是同一个，从而提出 Decoupled PPO 更新目标，把”用哪个策略采样”和”对哪个策略做 trust region”在形式上解耦开来。

  • GSPO 从 GRPO 在长序列和 MoE 模型上的稳定性问题出发，指出 token-level 的 PPO / GRPO 在专家路由高度波动（尤其是新旧策略之间的路由差异）时，会引入巨大的方差与不稳定。GSPO 提出在 sequence-level 定义 PPO-style 目标与比率约束，用整条序列的比率来约束更新，在 MoE 场景下显著缓解由路由不一致带来的训练崩溃。

  • Your Efficient RL Framework Secretly Brings You Off-Policy RL Training 关注现有的一些大模型 RL 训练框架（如 VeRL）中，推理框架和训练框架在同一功能模块上常常有不同实现（例如 vLLM 和 FSDP / Megatron 的算子差异），导致行为策略 ⟦INLMATH55MATHEND⟧ 与参考策略 ⟦INLMATH56MATHEND⟧ 不一致。这种不一致使得原本假定为同策略（on-policy）的训练，实际变成了带有明显偏差的异策略（off-policy）训练。文章总结了两种处理这一问题的现有方法：PPO-IS 与 vanilla-IS，并提出在 token-level 做截断重要性采样（truncated IS, TIS），减少训推不一致程度较重的样本对训练的影响。作者还写了两篇更基础的分析文章：Part I 和 Part II。

  • Small Leak Can Sink a Great Ship—Boost RL Training on MoE with 𝑰𝒄𝒆𝑷𝒐𝒑! 观察到，上述训推不一致问题在 MoE 模型上会被进一步放大：路由本身对微小扰动就极为敏感，叠加推理/训练实现差异与异步采样后，偏差很容易失控。文章提出 IcePop 方法：在 token-level 计算重要性采样比率，对过大或过小的比率做双侧掩码（masking），把这些”噪声较大”的数据从梯度中剔除，稳定 MoE 上的 RL 训练。

  • When Speed Kills Stability: Demystifying RL Collapse from the Training-Inference Mismatch 系统性分析了训推不一致的各种成因，包括智能体工作流中引入的大量分布外和低概率 token、硬件和内核/kernel 实现带来的计算不确定性；并分析了 token-level 重要性采样在长序列上为何会引入严重偏差。文章进一步提出 sequence-level 重要性采样掩码（sequence-level masked IS, sequence-level MIS）：只丢弃整条序列重要性比率过大的数据，在控制偏差的同时明显抑制由极端样本引发的训练崩溃。文中给出了较完整的理论推导和较充分的实验支撑。

  • verl Rollout Importance Sampling 在其 rollout correction 模块中引入 Token Veto（一票否决）机制：在 token-level 计算重要性比率 ⟦INLMATH57MATHEND⟧，若轨迹中存在任意 token 使得 ⟦INLMATH58MATHEND⟧，则把整条序列从训练中剔除。”token 粒度检测、sequence 粒度否决”体现的是一种”一票否决”的保守策略。

  • INTELLECT-3 Technical Report 在其异步分布式 RL 训练框架中采用了类似的拒绝采样策略。INTELLECT-3 对每条 rollout 计算 token-level 重要性比率，若任意 token 的比率低于阈值（文中取 ⟦INLMATH59MATHEND⟧），就对整条轨迹做 masking。

2.2 系统层：异步与训推对齐

  • AReaL 关注异步训练框架下行为策略与参考策略不一致的问题：rollout 往往由滞后的参数版本或不同 worker 产生。文章在异步框架下采用 Decoupled PPO 风格的目标，显式区分”行为策略分布”和”参考策略”，从而在异步场景下仍能维持类似 PPO 的优化性质。

  • Defeating Nondeterminism in LLM Inference 指出，批处理大小不变性（batch-size invariance）的缺失是大模型推理框架随机性的核心来源之一：同一个输入在不同的 batch 组合和 kernel 路径下，得到的概率分布会有明显差异。这意味着，即便”名义上”是同一套参数，真实运行时的行为策略 ⟦INLMATH60MATHEND⟧ 也会因系统负载和调度不同而波动，进一步放大训推不一致。

  • RL 老训崩？训推差异是基石 更多从实践角度出发，分享如何在实现上尽量靠近”训推一致”，包括选用一致的算子和精度配置、监控与约束训练端和推理端的 log-prob 偏差等，着重从训推框架层面入手，在工程上从源头缓解训推差异。

2.3 模型层：MoE 路由

  • Stabilizing MoE Reinforcement Learning by Aligning Training and Inference Routers 聚焦 MoE 架构下特有的 路由不一致（Routing Inconsistency） 问题。文章发现，即使输入完全相同，推理端和训练端也可能因为算子实现或并行的微小差异，让 Router 选中不同的专家。这种”物理路径”上的不一致，使行为策略 ⟦INLMATH61MATHEND⟧ 和参考策略 ⟦INLMATH62MATHEND⟧ 的差异远超预期，很容易触发训练崩溃。文章提出 Rollout Routing Replay (R3)：在推理阶段记录每个 token 实际命中的专家索引，在训练阶段强制回放这些路由决策，不再重新计算。R3 通过这种方式在 MoE 拓扑上强制对齐训推两端的计算路径。

3. 三策略 TRPO：最简统一框架

上述工作分属不同层面，但沿着”行为策略 vs 参考策略”这条主线整理，大部分都可以放进同一个最简框架：三策略 TRPO。

下面把推导压到只保留后文需要的结构。核心步骤只有两步：先写出以行为策略为基准的 surrogate gap，再用一次三角不等式把它改写成两个偏差来源。这个分解对分析训推不一致尤其有用：

  • 一方面帮助我们重新理解”训推不一致”和”异步训练框架”到底在影响什么；
  • 另一方面，也帮助我们统一理解 TIS、IcePop、sequence-level MIS 等方法；在本文的视角下，它们更多是在样本层面缓解”行为 vs 参考”偏移带来的估计偏差与方差问题，而不是直接改变 worst-case 意义下的 ⟦INLMATH63MATHEND⟧。

3.1 三个策略

沿用前文记号，在一个折扣 MDP 上工作，折扣因子为 ⟦INLMATH64MATHEND⟧：

对 LLM-RL 来说，更常见的往往是有限时域的序列决策。这里先沿用折扣 MDP 记号，只是为了直接承接经典 TRPO 的写法；后文的核心分解结构同样可以平移到有限时域版本。

  • 状态 ⟦INLMATH65MATHEND⟧，动作 ⟦INLMATH66MATHEND⟧；
  • 策略 ⟦INLMATH67MATHEND⟧；
  • 折扣状态分布： ⟦DISPMATH10MATHEND⟧
  • 回报（episode 视角）： ⟦DISPMATH11MATHEND⟧
  • 值函数 / 优势函数： ⟦DISPMATH12MATHEND⟧

沿用前文记号：行为策略为 ⟦INLMATH68MATHEND⟧，参考策略为 ⟦INLMATH69MATHEND⟧，目标策略为 ⟦INLMATH70MATHEND⟧。

理想设定下通常默认 ⟦INLMATH71MATHEND⟧；但在现实系统里二者往往不等，这就是”训推不一致”的数学体现。

3.2 两策略 TRPO：以行为策略为基准的 surrogate-gap 下界

熟悉 TRPO 的读者可以直接跳到后面的”三策略 TRPO”小节。

这里保留了 TRPO 的核心逻辑，但为了服务后文的三策略分解，我会用一条以行为策略 ⟦INLMATH72MATHEND⟧ 为基准、常数较松的 surrogate-gap 下界，而不是逐字复现 Schulman et al. (2015) 的原始定理形式。

TRPO 的理论保证都建立在某个”基准策略”的优势函数之上。本文直接把 ⟦INLMATH73MATHEND⟧ 当作基准：一是数据本就按 ⟦INLMATH74MATHEND⟧ 采样；二是在真实的 LLM-RL 里，我们通常只能用基于 ⟦INLMATH75MATHEND⟧ 数据估得的 critic / GAE / group-normalized reward 等 proxy 来近似 ⟦INLMATH76MATHEND⟧，这部分估计误差本文暂不纳入下界。

一个经典的结论是 性能差分引理（Performance Difference Lemma）：

对任意两策略 ⟦INLMATH77MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH78MATHEND⟧，有

⟦DISPMATH1MATHEND⟧

TRPO 的难点在于，无法准确计算

⟦DISPMATH13MATHEND⟧

因为 ⟦INLMATH79MATHEND⟧ 是”新策略”的状态分布，而我们并没有在这个分布下采样。

于是 TRPO 引入一个代理目标：把状态分布换成行为策略的：

⟦DISPMATH14MATHEND⟧

实际计算时，⟦INLMATH80MATHEND⟧ 往往通过重要性采样改写成基于行为策略样本的形式；这里先保留更直接的定义，后文再回到 PPO 风格的实现写法。

注意：这里的理论 surrogate ⟦INLMATH81MATHEND⟧ 是以行为策略 ⟦INLMATH82MATHEND⟧ 为基准定义的；而实际 PPO / GRPO 风格的 loss 往往把 ratio 的分母写成参考策略 ⟦INLMATH83MATHEND⟧。这两者之间正隔着一层”行为 vs 参考”的不一致，也正是下文引入 ⟦INLMATH84MATHEND⟧ 的原因。

从性能差分引理出发，两者之差是：

⟦DISPMATH15MATHEND⟧

定义

⟦DISPMATH16MATHEND⟧

便有一个直接的上界：

引理 1（状态分布偏移项的直接上界）

⟦DISPMATH2MATHEND⟧

这里出现了第一个关键量：

状态分布偏移 ⟦INLMATH85MATHEND⟧，也就是”新策略和行为策略看到的世界到底差了多少”。

通常不会直接约束 ⟦INLMATH86MATHEND⟧，而是约束”每一步 action 分布”上的差异，比如 trust region、KL、clip 等。

记总变差距离（total variation）：

⟦DISPMATH17MATHEND⟧

假设存在常数 ⟦INLMATH87MATHEND⟧，使得

对所有 ⟦INLMATH88MATHEND⟧，行为策略与目标策略之间的 TV 被 ⟦INLMATH89MATHEND⟧ 上界：

⟦DISPMATH3MATHEND⟧

直观含义：在任意状态下，”新策略”和”生成数据的策略”选动作的分布都不会离太远。

一个经典结果（用 coupling 可证）是：

引理 2（策略 TV 到状态分布偏移的传播界） 在上述条件下有

⟦DISPMATH4MATHEND⟧

把它和引理 1 结合：

⟦DISPMATH18MATHEND⟧

于是得到一条形式上很简洁的以行为策略为基准的两策略 surrogate-gap 下界：

定理 1（以行为策略为基准的两策略下界）

⟦DISPMATH5MATHEND⟧

这说明：

  • 真正决定”代理目标 ⟦INLMATH90MATHEND⟧ 靠不靠谱”的，是行为策略 ⟦INLMATH91MATHEND⟧ 与目标策略 ⟦INLMATH92MATHEND⟧ 的差异： ⟦DISPMATH19MATHEND⟧

只要能直接约束住这个 ⟦INLMATH93MATHEND⟧，就能把 TRPO 的单调性保证搬到行为策略视角下。这里采用便于展示的 worst-case TV 写法，常数不求最紧，只求把结构写清楚；若改用 average-TV 版本，也能得到更贴近样本平均的类似结论，只是常数和期望形式会相应变化。

3.3 三策略 TRPO

现实问题在于：在大模型 RL 训练里，我们通常无法直接控制 ⟦INLMATH94MATHEND⟧ 本身。

在大部分 PPO / GRPO / GSPO 及现有 RLHF 框架中，实际发生的是：

  • rollout 数据由某个行为策略 ⟦INLMATH95MATHEND⟧ 产生（推理引擎中的”那一版参数”加上若干系统细节）；
  • 更新时希望用参考策略 ⟦INLMATH96MATHEND⟧ 来限制目标策略 ⟦INLMATH97MATHEND⟧ 的更新幅度。

也就是说，实际能直接控制或间接影响的是两个量：

  1. 参考 vs 目标：可以通过 KL / clip 等手段控制 ⟦DISPMATH20MATHEND⟧
  2. 行为 vs 参考：希望间接控制 ⟦DISPMATH21MATHEND⟧

于是自然就得到两个偏差来源，对应两个 TV 距离量：

  • 偏差源 A：参考 vs 目标 ⟦DISPMATH22MATHEND⟧
  • 偏差源 B：行为 vs 参考 ⟦DISPMATH23MATHEND⟧

直觉上：

  • ⟦INLMATH98MATHEND⟧：新策略离训练里选定的参考策略有多远——这就是 trust region 控制的部分；
  • ⟦INLMATH99MATHEND⟧：训练用的参考策略与真实采样时的行为策略差多少——这就是训推不一致或异步的影子。

现在把这两个量代回 TRPO 的下界。

对任意状态 ⟦INLMATH100MATHEND⟧，有

⟦DISPMATH24MATHEND⟧

对 ⟦INLMATH101MATHEND⟧ 取上确界：

⟦DISPMATH25MATHEND⟧

把这个不等式代回定理 1，记

⟦DISPMATH26MATHEND⟧

就有

⟦DISPMATH27MATHEND⟧

于是得到一条直接的保守三策略 TRPO 下界（也是定理 1 的直接推论）：

定理 2（三策略 TRPO） 记

⟦DISPMATH6MATHEND⟧

以及

⟦DISPMATH7MATHEND⟧

则对任意目标策略 ⟦INLMATH102MATHEND⟧ 有

⟦DISPMATH8MATHEND⟧

其中

⟦DISPMATH9MATHEND⟧

定理 2 并不复杂：⟦INLMATH103MATHEND⟧ 与 ⟦INLMATH104MATHEND⟧ 之间的 gap 不是被精确拆成两项，而是被一条保守上界控制；这条上界恰好由”参考 vs 目标”的 ⟦INLMATH105MATHEND⟧ 和”行为 vs 参考”的 ⟦INLMATH106MATHEND⟧ 共同决定。若要真正推出性能提升，还需要 ⟦INLMATH107MATHEND⟧ 本身足够高，例如至少要超过 ⟦INLMATH108MATHEND⟧。在 LLM 场景里，这个界的数值通常很松，所以我更把它当作一个结构工具，而不是性能证书。

3.4 这两个偏差来源各自怎么控制？

回头看各种实际方法：

  • 大多数 PPO / GRPO 类工作，主要是在控制”参考 vs 目标“这一侧的偏移，也就是 ⟦INLMATH109MATHEND⟧；
  • GSPO 的方向也在这一侧，但它不只是把 ⟦INLMATH110MATHEND⟧ 控制得更稳，而是进一步把 ratio、clipping 和优化的基本单位从 token-level 改到 sequence-level，从而改变了这类偏移被度量和约束的粒度；
  • TIS / IcePop / MIS / WTRS 则更多是在样本权重、样本子集或拒绝规则层面，缓解”行为 vs 参考“偏移带来的估计偏差与方差问题，而不是直接改变 ⟦INLMATH111MATHEND⟧ 的 worst-case 定义值。

至于我更关心哪一侧，基本总是后者：在真实系统里，trust region 通常还没先坏掉，行为策略和参考策略的失配就已经足以把训练拖离”近似 on-policy”的区域。本文下面只讨论”行为 vs 参考“这一侧。

这一侧的目标是：让真正参与训练的数据尽量来自”接近参考策略”的行为分布，或至少不要让严重不一致的样本主导梯度。

这里通常既有系统层的机制，也有算法层（importance sampling）的机制。

  1. 系统层：让行为策略与参考策略保持接近
    • 异步框架：给每个样本打上策略版本号，只使用与 ⟦INLMATH112MATHEND⟧ 相差不大的参数版本采样的数据；
    • 训推对齐：让训练框架和推理框架用相同的精度、算子和相近的内核/kernel 行为。

    这些机制的目标是：从”算法外部”让 ⟦INLMATH113MATHEND⟧ 和 ⟦INLMATH114MATHEND⟧ 靠近，从而直接压低 ⟦INLMATH115MATHEND⟧。

  2. 算法层：样本修正

    在算法层，我们不再试图”纠正整个行为策略”，而是用重要性采样比率在样本层面做筛选和重加权，让真正参与训练的样本子集更接近参考策略，或减小差异较大的样本在训练中的权重。

    具体就是下面这些方法。它们的共同点是：都在样本层面缓解”行为 vs 参考”偏移带来的不良后果。

4. 重要性采样与掩码：四种围绕”行为 vs 参考”偏移的样本级机制

下面沿用前文的记号给出这四种方法的目标函数，只聚焦”行为策略 vs 参考策略”这一维。这里的统一写法主要突出”行为 vs 参考”偏移在训练 loss 中是如何被处理的，并不逐字复现各原论文中的所有实现细节（如优势估计、baseline 和额外正则项）。下面各个 ⟦INLMATH116MATHEND⟧ 也只是为了方便比较而写出的训练 loss 抽象，不应理解为理论 surrogate ⟦INLMATH117MATHEND⟧ 的逐字无偏估计。记 token 级的 PPO / GRPO 风格更新项为

⟦DISPMATH28MATHEND⟧

其中

⟦DISPMATH29MATHEND⟧

  • ⟦INLMATH118MATHEND⟧ 是 目标 vs 参考 的比率；
  • ⟦INLMATH119MATHEND⟧ 表示与理论上的 ⟦INLMATH120MATHEND⟧ 对应的优势项；实践里它通常来自基于 ⟦INLMATH121MATHEND⟧ 数据估得的 critic / GAE / group-normalized reward 等 proxy，因此不应理解为对 ⟦INLMATH122MATHEND⟧ 的逐字无偏替代。

为了把 token 级的 ⟦INLMATH123MATHEND⟧ 与序列级的 ⟦INLMATH124MATHEND⟧ 记号打通，在以 RLHF（reinforcement learning from human feedback，人类反馈强化学习）为代表的 LLM-RL 设定下，我们约定：

  • prompt 记为 ⟦INLMATH125MATHEND⟧；回复记为 ⟦INLMATH126MATHEND⟧；
  • token 级状态 ⟦INLMATH127MATHEND⟧，动作 ⟦INLMATH128MATHEND⟧；
  • 因此行为策略和参考策略在序列上的分布可写成 ⟦DISPMATH30MATHEND⟧

此外，为描述”参考 vs 行为”的偏移，统一定义 token 级重要性比率

⟦DISPMATH31MATHEND⟧

以及其对应的序列级版本

⟦DISPMATH32MATHEND⟧

接下来，TIS / IcePop / MIS 的区别就在于”如何用这些 ⟦INLMATH129MATHEND⟧ 缓解行为 vs 参考偏移带来的训练问题”。

4.1 TIS：token-level 截断 IS

TIS 直接对上述 ⟦INLMATH130MATHEND⟧ 做截断，记

⟦DISPMATH33MATHEND⟧

更新目标写成

⟦DISPMATH34MATHEND⟧

  • 蓝色的 ⟦INLMATH131MATHEND⟧ 是被截断的 IS 权重：极端大的比率被压到常数 ⟦INLMATH132MATHEND⟧。
  • 从三策略 TRPO 的角度看，这相当于在 token 样本层面“软削弱”行为策略与参考策略严重不一致的样本，减小它们对有效训练分布的影响。
